name: The Ridges
overview: The Ridges is the crown jewel of Summerlin and one of the most exclusive residential communities in all of Nevada. Located within a 793-acre guard-gated enclave along the western rim of the Las Vegas Valley, The Ridges backs directly against the Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area, providing dramatic desert canyon views from virtually every estate.

Developed by the Howard Hughes Corporation beginning in 2006, The Ridges was designed from the ground up as Summerlin's ultra-luxury showcase. The community is anchored by Bear's Best Las Vegas, a Jack Nicklaus-designed golf course that features replicas of 18 of the greatest holes Nicklaus has ever designed. Membership is available but not required for homeownership.

Homes in The Ridges range from approximately $2 million for resale semi-custom homes to over $20 million for new-build custom estates on premium lots with unobstructed Red Rock views. The architectural standards are among the strictest in Las Vegas, resulting in a cohesive desert contemporary aesthetic throughout the community.

The guard-gated entry, 24-hour security, and strict HOA governance create a level of privacy and exclusivity that attracts high-net-worth individuals, executives, professional athletes, and entertainment industry figures. The Ridges consistently commands the highest per-square-foot prices in the Las Vegas metro area.

highlights.body: Jack Nicklaus-designed 18-hole championship course featuring replicas of his greatest holes. Private membership available to residents.

highlights.body: 24-hour staffed guard gate with comprehensive security patrols. One of the most secure residential communities in Nevada.

highlights.body: Homes back directly against the Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area with unobstructed views of the canyon's dramatic sandstone formations.

highlights.title: Custom Estate Living
highlights.body: Semi-custom and full custom homes ranging from 4,000 to 15,000+ square feet with the strictest architectural standards in Las Vegas.

highlights.title: Premier Location
highlights.body: 10 minutes to Red Rock Canyon, 20 minutes to the Strip, and adjacent to Downtown Summerlin's 125+ shops and restaurants.

highlights.title: Elite Investment Value
highlights.body: The Ridges consistently commands the highest per-square-foot prices in the Las Vegas metro, with strong appreciation through every market cycle.

faqs.a: Homes in The Ridges range from approximately $2 million for resale semi-custom homes to over $20 million for new-build custom estates on premium lots with unobstructed Red Rock Canyon views.

faqs.q: Is The Ridges guard-gated?
faqs.a: Yes. The Ridges is a fully guard-gated community with a 24-hour staffed guard gate and comprehensive security patrols. It is one of the most secure residential communities in Nevada.

faqs.q: What golf course is in The Ridges?
faqs.a: Bear's Best Las Vegas is a Jack Nicklaus-designed 18-hole championship course located within The Ridges. It features replicas of 18 of the greatest holes Nicklaus has ever designed.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees in The Ridges?
faqs.a: HOA fees in The Ridges typically range from $500 to $1,200 per month, which includes the master Summerlin association fee plus The Ridges sub-association fee. Fees cover guard gate staffing, security patrols, common area maintenance, and community amenities.

faqs.q: What schools serve The Ridges?
faqs.a: The Ridges is served by CCSD schools including Sig Rogich Middle School (10/10 GreatSchools) and Palo Verde High School (8/10). Private options include The Meadows School (A+) and Bishop Gorman High School (A+).

faqs.q: Is The Ridges in Las Vegas or Henderson?
faqs.a: The Ridges is located in Summerlin, which is part of the City of Las Vegas and unincorporated Clark County. It is not part of Henderson.

faqs.q: Who built homes in The Ridges?
faqs.a: The Ridges was master-developed by the Howard Hughes Corporation. Homes include both semi-custom builds by national builders and full custom estates by private architects and luxury builders like Blue Heron, Sun West Custom Homes, and others.

name: Ascaya
overview: Ascaya is one of the most exclusive luxury communities in the American Southwest. Set on 313 acres along the McCullough Range ridgeline in Henderson, the community occupies a dramatic elevated position that provides panoramic views of the entire Las Vegas Valley, the Strip skyline, and the surrounding mountain ranges. Every lot was engineered for unobstructed views, and the desert-modern architectural standards are among the most exacting in Nevada.

Originally entitled in the mid-2000s, the community went through development pauses during the recession before being revitalized with fresh investment and infrastructure. Ascaya's 313 custom lots range from approximately one-third of an acre to over an acre, with finished pad sites, underground utilities, and paved streets already in place. Buyers purchase a lot and then work with an approved architect and builder to create a fully custom home.

Blue Heron is the most prominent builder at Ascaya, known for sleek desert-contemporary design with disappearing glass walls, negative-edge pools, and dramatic indoor-outdoor living spaces. Finished homes at Ascaya range from approximately $3 million to well over $20 million, with several estates exceeding 10,000 square feet. The community clubhouse features a resort-style pool, fitness center, and private event space.

Ascaya sits just minutes from Henderson's executive airport, the shops and restaurants of The District at Green Valley Ranch, and Interstate 215 for a direct corridor to the Strip and Harry Reid International Airport. The combination of extreme privacy, architectural freedom, and some of the best panoramic views in the Las Vegas Valley makes Ascaya a top destination for ultra-high-net-worth buyers seeking a one-of-a-kind estate.

highlights.body: Every lot at Ascaya was engineered on the McCullough Range ridgeline for panoramic views of the Las Vegas Valley, the Strip, and surrounding mountains. Lots range from 1/3 to 1+ acre.

highlights.body: 24-hour staffed guard gate with advanced security infrastructure. One of the most private and secure residential enclaves in the American Southwest.

highlights.body: The Ascaya Clubhouse features a resort-style infinity pool overlooking the valley, fitness center, private event space, and concierge services for residents.

highlights.title: Exceptional Investment
highlights.body: Ascaya commands some of the highest lot and per-square-foot prices in Nevada. Limited inventory and irreplaceable views support long-term value appreciation.

name: Sun City Anthem
overview: Sun City Anthem is one of the most acclaimed 55+ active adult communities in the United States. Developed by Del Webb beginning in 1998 and spanning approximately 2,400 acres in southeast Henderson, the community is home to over 7,200 residences built around two championship golf courses, multiple recreation centers, and one of the most active social calendars in the Las Vegas Valley.

The community features three recreation centers totaling over 100,000 square feet of amenity space. The Anthem Center serves as the primary hub with resort-style pools, a fitness center, tennis and pickleball courts, a ballroom, and meeting rooms for the 100+ chartered clubs and organizations. The Liberty Center and Independence Center provide additional fitness, arts and crafts, and social gathering spaces spread throughout the community.

Two golf courses anchor the community: the Anthem Course and the Revere Golf Club at Anthem, offering championship-caliber desert golf with views of the McCullough Range. The community's trail system connects to Henderson's broader network, providing miles of walking and biking paths through the foothills terrain. Outdoor amphitheaters, community gardens, and sports courts round out the amenity package.

Homes in Sun City Anthem range from approximately $350,000 for well-maintained single-story villas to $700,000 or more for larger estate-style homes on premium lots with golf course or mountain views. The community's location in southeast Henderson provides easy access to shopping, dining, medical facilities, Lake Mead, and the I-215 beltway corridor for travel to the Strip and airport.

highlights.body: Over 100,000 square feet of amenity space across the Anthem Center, Liberty Center, and Independence Center. Resort pools, fitness, ballrooms, and arts studios.

highlights.body: The Anthem Course and Revere Golf Club at Anthem offer desert championship golf with mountain views. Preferred rates and tee times for residents.

highlights.body: From pickleball leagues to art guilds to travel clubs, Sun City Anthem's social calendar is one of the most active of any 55+ community in the country.

highlights.body: Virtually every home in Sun City Anthem is single-story, designed for comfort and accessibility. Floor plans range from 1,200 to 3,500+ square feet.

highlights.title: Henderson Safety
highlights.body: Henderson consistently ranks among the top 10 safest large cities in the United States. Sun City Anthem benefits from the city's low crime rates and responsive services.

highlights.title: Strong Resale Value
highlights.body: Del Webb communities hold value well due to strong demand from retiring Baby Boomers. Sun City Anthem's amenity package and Henderson location drive consistent resale performance.

name: Anthem Country Club
overview: Anthem Country Club is the premier guard-gated luxury community within the broader Anthem master plan in Henderson. Anchored by an 18-hole championship golf course designed by Hale Irwin and Keith Foster, the community occupies some of the highest elevation in the Anthem foothills, delivering panoramic views of the Las Vegas Strip, the Spring Mountains, and the McCullough Range from virtually every neighborhood.

The private country club features a 72-par championship course that plays through natural desert terrain at elevations ranging from 2,900 to 3,400 feet. The clubhouse offers full-service dining, a resort-style pool, tennis courts, a fitness center, and a full calendar of member events. Club membership is separate from homeownership and available in golf, social, and fitness tiers.

Homes in Anthem Country Club range from approximately $1.2 million for semi-custom homes in established neighborhoods to over $8 million for custom estates on premium lots with direct golf course and Strip views. The community features a mix of production luxury homes by Toll Brothers, semi-custom builds, and full custom estates. Architectural standards emphasize desert-Mediterranean and contemporary styles.

The guard-gated entry with 24-hour staffing provides security and exclusivity while maintaining easy access to Henderson's infrastructure. The I-215 beltway is minutes away, providing a clean commute corridor to the Strip, Harry Reid Airport, and the rest of the valley. Lake Mead is approximately 25 minutes east, and the Galleria at Sunset shopping center is a 10-minute drive.

highlights.body: 18-hole championship golf course designed by Hale Irwin and Keith Foster. 72-par desert course playing through natural terrain at elevations up to 3,400 feet with panoramic Strip views.

highlights.body: 24-hour staffed guard gate with full security infrastructure. One of Henderson's most secure and private luxury communities with controlled access.

highlights.body: The elevated foothills position delivers direct views of the Las Vegas Strip skyline, particularly dramatic at sunset and after dark from higher-elevation lots.

highlights.body: Private clubhouse with resort-style pool, full-service dining, tennis courts, fitness center, spa services, and a year-round calendar of member events and social programming.

highlights.title: Custom Estate Opportunities
highlights.body: A mix of production luxury homes by Toll Brothers, semi-custom builds, and full custom estates. Homes range from 3,000 to 10,000+ square feet on lots up to one acre.

highlights.title: Henderson's Safest City
highlights.body: Henderson is consistently ranked among the top 10 safest large cities in America. Anthem Country Club benefits from both city services and its own guard-gated security.

name: The Summit Club
overview: The Summit Club is the most exclusive residential community in Las Vegas and one of the most prestigious private clubs in the American West. Developed by Discovery Land Company on 555 acres within Summerlin, The Summit Club features a Tom Fazio-designed championship golf course, an ultra-luxury clubhouse, and just 146 homesites reserved for members and their guests.

Discovery Land Company is known for creating the world's most exclusive private residential communities, including Yellowstone Club in Montana, Kukio in Hawaii, and Baker's Bay in the Bahamas. The Summit Club brings that same level of exclusivity, design, and service to the Las Vegas market. Membership is by invitation only, and the initiation fees are among the highest of any private club in the country.

The Tom Fazio-designed golf course plays through natural desert terrain with dramatic elevation changes and unobstructed views of Red Rock Canyon, the Spring Mountains, and the Las Vegas Strip. The clubhouse complex includes a full-service restaurant, resort-style pool and cabana village, a fitness and wellness center, indoor and outdoor tennis courts, a children's activity center, and concierge services.

Homesites at The Summit Club range from one-third of an acre to over two acres. Finished homes range from approximately $5 million for smaller custom builds to over $30 million for the largest estates. The architectural palette emphasizes desert-contemporary design with natural stone, glass, and steel. Homes are built by approved builders including Shapiro & Sher, Sun West Custom Homes, and other luxury firms.

highlights.body: 18-hole championship course by Tom Fazio playing through dramatic desert terrain with elevation changes and views of Red Rock Canyon, the Spring Mountains, and the Las Vegas Strip.

highlights.title: Discovery Land Company
highlights.body: Developed by Discovery Land Company, creators of the world's most exclusive private communities including Yellowstone Club, Kukio, and Baker's Bay. Invitation-only membership.

highlights.title: 146 Homesites
highlights.body: Only 146 homesites on 555 acres, creating an ultra-low-density residential environment with maximum privacy and space. The most exclusive address in Las Vegas.

highlights.title: Ultra-Luxury Clubhouse
highlights.body: Full-service clubhouse with fine dining, resort-style pool and cabana village, fitness and wellness center, tennis courts, children's center, and concierge services.

highlights.title: Red Rock Canyon Setting
highlights.body: Located along the western rim of Summerlin with direct views of Red Rock Canyon's sandstone formations. Adjacent to the 195,000-acre conservation area.

highlights.title: Extreme Exclusivity
highlights.body: Membership by invitation only with initiation fees among the highest of any private club in the country. Security and privacy rival any residential community in the world.

neighborhoods.desc: Homesites along the Tom Fazio fairways with direct golf course views and mountain backdrops. The most popular position for avid golfers.

name: Sun City Summerlin
overview: Sun City Summerlin is the original Del Webb active adult community in the Las Vegas Valley and remains one of the most popular 55+ communities in the American West. Built between 1989 and 2002 on approximately 2,400 acres along the western rim of the valley, the community is home to 7,779 residences and an amenity package that rivals small resort towns.

The community features four recreation centers — Mountain Shadows, Desert Vista, Sun Shadows, and Pinnacle — totaling over 120,000 square feet of indoor and outdoor amenity space. Residents have access to three golf courses, including Highland Falls Golf Club, Palm Valley Golf Club, and Eagle Crest Golf Club. The community also features over 100 chartered clubs, indoor and outdoor pools, tennis and pickleball courts, fitness centers, a library, woodworking shops, and an outdoor amphitheater.

Sun City Summerlin occupies one of the best locations in the Las Vegas Valley. The western edge of the community borders Red Rock Canyon, providing hiking trail access directly from the neighborhood. Downtown Summerlin's 125+ shops and restaurants are within a five-minute drive. The Summerlin Hospital Medical Center is adjacent to the community, and the Summerlin Parkway provides a direct commute corridor to the Strip and downtown.

Homes range from approximately $300,000 for well-maintained two-bedroom villas to $700,000 or more for larger estate-style homes on premium lots with golf course or mountain views. All homes are single-story. The original Del Webb construction is well-built with block walls and tile roofs, and many homes have been extensively renovated with modern finishes. The community's maturity means established landscaping, settled HOAs, and a proven track record of resale value.

highlights.title: Three Golf Courses
highlights.body: Highland Falls, Palm Valley, and Eagle Crest offer 54 holes of golf within the community. Residents enjoy preferred tee times and rates. Some of the best mountain views in the valley from the fairways.

highlights.title: Four Recreation Centers
highlights.body: Mountain Shadows, Desert Vista, Sun Shadows, and Pinnacle provide 120,000+ square feet of pools, fitness, ballrooms, arts studios, woodworking shops, and meeting rooms.

highlights.title: 100+ Chartered Clubs
highlights.body: One of the most active social calendars of any 55+ community in the country. Clubs for every interest from pickleball to photography to travel groups to theater.

highlights.title: Red Rock Canyon Access
highlights.body: The western edge of Sun City Summerlin borders Red Rock Canyon with direct trail access from the community. Hiking and biking without leaving the neighborhood.

highlights.title: Adjacent to Downtown Summerlin
highlights.body: 125+ shops and restaurants at Downtown Summerlin are a five-minute drive. Summerlin Hospital Medical Center is immediately adjacent to the community.

highlights.title: Proven 35-Year Track Record
highlights.body: Built starting in 1989, Sun City Summerlin has a 35-year track record of stable property values, settled HOAs, and a proven community governance model.

name: Green Valley
overview: Green Valley is Henderson's original master-planned community and one of the first large-scale planned developments in the Las Vegas Valley. Developed by the American Nevada Corporation beginning in 1978, the community spans approximately 8,400 acres and is home to over 35,000 residences ranging from entry-level condominiums to luxury guard-gated estates.

The community is organized into distinct neighborhoods and sub-communities that were built over several decades. Green Valley proper includes the original neighborhoods from the late 1970s and 1980s, while Green Valley Ranch and Green Valley South represent later phases with newer construction and expanded amenities. The District at Green Valley Ranch serves as the community's retail and dining hub with 40+ shops and restaurants in an open-air setting.

Green Valley's maturity is one of its greatest assets. Established landscaping, tree-lined streets, mature parks, and a proven track record of stable property values give the community a settled, permanent feel that newer developments cannot replicate. The community also benefits from some of the strongest public schools in the Las Vegas Valley, with several elementary and middle schools earning top ratings.

The location in the heart of Henderson provides easy access to the I-215 beltway, which serves as the primary commute corridor to the Strip, the airport, and the rest of the valley. Lake Mead is approximately 20 minutes east. The Galleria at Sunset, St. Rose Dominican Hospital, and the Henderson city government campus are all within or immediately adjacent to Green Valley.

highlights.title: Henderson's Original Master Plan
highlights.body: Founded in 1978 by the American Nevada Corporation. The community that put Henderson on the map and established it as a premier residential city in the Las Vegas Valley.

highlights.title: Top-Rated Schools
highlights.body: Green Valley is served by some of the highest-rated public schools in the Las Vegas Valley, including multiple elementary and middle schools with 8/10 and above GreatSchools ratings.

highlights.title: The District at GVR
highlights.body: The District at Green Valley Ranch offers 40+ shops and restaurants in an open-air lifestyle center with fountains, outdoor seating, and regular community events.

highlights.title: Mature Landscaping & Parks
highlights.body: Decades of growth have created tree-lined streets, established parks, and a lush residential feel that newer Las Vegas communities cannot replicate.

highlights.title: Every Price Point
highlights.body: From $350K condominiums to $3M+ luxury estates, Green Valley offers genuine housing diversity across entry-level, family, move-up, and luxury segments.

highlights.title: Henderson Safety
highlights.body: Green Valley is located in Henderson, consistently ranked among the top 10 safest large cities in the United States. Low crime, clean streets, and responsive city services.

faqs.a: Homes in Green Valley range from approximately $350,000 for condominiums and entry-level single-family homes to over $3 million for luxury estates in guard-gated enclaves like Green Valley Highlands and golf course communities.

faqs.a: Yes. Green Valley is served by some of the highest-rated public schools in the Las Vegas Valley, including Nate Mack Elementary (9/10), Elise L. Wolff Elementary (9/10), Bob Miller Middle School (8/10), and Green Valley High School (7/10).

faqs.a: Green Valley Ranch is a large section within the broader Green Valley community. Green Valley Ranch was developed later, primarily in the 1990s and 2000s, and has its own retail center, parks, and resort-casino.

faqs.q: What is The District at Green Valley Ranch?
faqs.a: The District is an open-air shopping and dining center with 40+ shops and restaurants, fountains, outdoor seating, and regular community events. It serves as the primary retail hub for Green Valley Ranch and the surrounding neighborhoods.

faqs.q: When was Green Valley established?
faqs.a: Green Valley was established in 1978 by the American Nevada Corporation. It was one of the first large-scale master-planned communities in the Las Vegas Valley and is credited with establishing Henderson as a premier residential city.

faqs.q: Is Green Valley guard-gated?
faqs.a: Most of Green Valley is not guard-gated. However, several luxury sub-communities within Green Valley, including Green Valley Highlands and portions of Green Valley Ranch, are guard-gated with 24-hour security.

name: The Paseos
overview: The Paseos is one of Summerlin's most sought-after family villages, located in the western portion of the master plan along the base of the Spring Mountains. Developed by the Howard Hughes Corporation beginning in 2004, the village spans approximately 500 acres and is home to over 2,800 residences organized around an extensive network of parks, trails, and green spaces.

The village earned its name from the paseo trail system that winds throughout the community, connecting neighborhoods to parks, schools, and open spaces. Fox Hill Park, one of Summerlin's signature parks, is located within The Paseos and features zip lines, an adventure playground, disc golf, and miles of trails. The park system gives The Paseos a resort-like atmosphere that sets it apart from other Summerlin villages.

Families are drawn to The Paseos for its school zones. The village feeds into some of the highest-rated schools in the CCSD system, including consistently top-ranked elementary and middle schools. The proximity to The Meadows School and other private options in western Summerlin adds further educational appeal.

Homes in The Paseos range from approximately $550,000 for established single-family homes to $1.2 million or more for larger estate-style homes on premium lots. Builders who have built in The Paseos include Toll Brothers, Pulte Homes, KB Home, and Richmond American. The village's location provides easy access to Downtown Summerlin, Red Rock Canyon, and the Summerlin Parkway commute corridor.

highlights.body: Summerlin's signature adventure park with zip lines, climbing structures, disc golf, and miles of trails. A destination park that draws visitors from across the valley.

highlights.title: Top-Rated Schools
highlights.body: The Paseos feeds into some of the highest-rated CCSD schools in the valley. Families consistently cite school zones as a primary reason for choosing this village.

highlights.body: An extensive network of walking and biking trails connecting neighborhoods, parks, and schools. The trail system gives the village a resort-like atmosphere.

highlights.title: Family-Focused Design
highlights.body: The village was designed from the ground up for family living with safe streets, cul-de-sacs, pocket parks, and community gathering spaces integrated throughout.

highlights.title: Downtown Summerlin Access
highlights.body: Five minutes to Downtown Summerlin's 125+ shops, restaurants, the Las Vegas Ballpark, and the Red Rock Resort Casino.

highlights.title: Red Rock Canyon Proximity
highlights.body: Ten minutes to Red Rock Canyon for hiking, biking, and outdoor recreation. The Spring Mountains provide a dramatic western backdrop visible from many homes.

name: The Peaks
overview: The Peaks is Summerlin's newest guard-gated luxury village, positioned in the western reaches of the master plan along the base of the Spring Mountains. Developed by the Howard Hughes Corporation beginning in 2018, The Peaks represents the latest evolution of luxury living in Summerlin, offering new-construction custom and semi-custom homes with contemporary desert architecture and modern floor plans.

The guard-gated village features multiple neighborhoods developed by premier builders including Toll Brothers, William Lyon Homes, and Shea Homes. Homes range from approximately $1 million for production luxury residences to over $4 million for custom estates on premium lots. The architectural standards emphasize clean desert-contemporary lines, natural stone and stucco exteriors, and open floor plans with indoor-outdoor living.

The Peaks benefits from its position at the highest elevations within Summerlin, providing dramatic views of Red Rock Canyon to the west and the Las Vegas Valley and Strip to the east. The village is connected to Summerlin's extensive trail system, with direct access to hiking and biking paths leading toward Red Rock Canyon.

As one of the newest villages in Summerlin, The Peaks offers the advantage of modern construction with current building codes, energy-efficient systems, smart-home technology, and contemporary finishes. The village's proximity to Downtown Summerlin, The Ridges, and Red Rock Canyon creates a premium location within an already premium master plan.

name: Sunstone
overview: Sunstone is one of North Las Vegas's newest master-planned communities, launched in 2019 on approximately 1,200 acres in the upper northwest corridor of the city. When fully built out, the community will contain approximately 3,650 homes across multiple neighborhoods being developed by national homebuilders including Lennar, KB Home, Century Communities, and Richmond American Homes.

The community was designed around a central park and trail system that connects neighborhoods to recreation areas, schools, and open spaces. The Sunstone community park features a playground, sports courts, walking trails, and open turf areas. Future phases of the community will add additional parks, a community center, and expanded trail connections.

Sunstone appeals to first-time buyers, young families, and move-up buyers seeking new construction at price points significantly below comparable new homes in Summerlin or Henderson. Homes range from approximately $380,000 for single-story starter homes to $650,000 for larger two-story family homes with upgraded finishes. The community's active builder environment means buyers can select from multiple floor plans and lot positions.

The community's location in upper North Las Vegas provides access to the I-15 and US-95 freeway corridors for commuting to the Strip, downtown Las Vegas, and other employment centers. Aliante Casino & Hotel and the Aliante Nature Discovery Park are nearby. While the surrounding area is still developing, the rapid growth of North Las Vegas's northwest corridor is bringing new retail, dining, and services to the Sunstone area.

highlights.body: New-build homes from national builders at price points $100K-$200K below comparable new construction in Summerlin or Henderson. Strong value for first-time and move-up buyers.

highlights.body: Multiple national builders including Lennar, KB Home, Century Communities, and Richmond American. Wide selection of floor plans, lot positions, and upgrade options.

highlights.title: Freeway Access

name: Dragon Rock
overview: Dragon Rock is the most exclusive enclave within MacDonald Highlands, a double guard-gated collection of approximately 30 custom homesites developed by Blue Heron on some of the most dramatic terrain in the Las Vegas Valley. Named for the dramatic rock formations visible from the community, Dragon Rock occupies a premium elevated position within the already-elevated MacDonald Highlands master plan, delivering panoramic views of the Strip, the valley, and the surrounding mountain ranges.

Blue Heron is the sole builder and developer of Dragon Rock, bringing their signature desert-contemporary aesthetic to every home. Blue Heron's design philosophy emphasizes disappearing glass walls, negative-edge pools, natural stone, and seamless indoor-outdoor living. Homes range from approximately $5 million to over $15 million, with finished residences typically spanning 6,000 to 12,000+ square feet.

The double guard-gated security means residents pass through the MacDonald Highlands guard gate first, then through the private Dragon Rock gate — creating two layers of controlled access. This arrangement delivers an exceptional level of privacy and exclusivity, attracting high-net-worth individuals, executives, and celebrities who value discretion above all else.

Dragon Rock residents have access to the DragonRidge Country Club, which features a Jay Morrish and David Druzisky-designed 18-hole championship course, a full-service clubhouse with dining and events, resort-style pools, tennis courts, and a fitness center. Membership is separate from homeownership. The community's location in Henderson provides access to I-215 for a direct corridor to the Strip, the airport, and Lake Mead.

highlights.body: Two layers of guard-gated security: the MacDonald Highlands gate followed by the private Dragon Rock gate. One of the most secure residential enclaves in the American West.

highlights.body: Blue Heron is the sole developer and builder, creating award-winning desert-contemporary estates with disappearing glass walls, negative-edge pools, and museum-quality finishes.

highlights.body: Access to a Jay Morrish-designed 18-hole championship course, full-service clubhouse, resort pools, tennis, and fitness. One of Henderson's premier private clubs.

highlights.body: The elevated position within MacDonald Highlands provides 180-degree panoramic views of the Las Vegas Strip, the valley, and surrounding mountain ranges.

highlights.body: Only 30 homesites in the entire community, creating extreme exclusivity and privacy. Each lot was positioned for maximum views and minimum visual intrusion from neighbors.

highlights.body: Blue Heron homes at Dragon Rock have won multiple architecture and design awards. The Desert Contemporary aesthetic has become a defining style of Las Vegas luxury.

name: Ascension at The Peaks
overview: Ascension at The Peaks is the newest guard-gated luxury community within Summerlin's prestigious Peaks village, representing one of the most significant new residential developments in the Las Vegas Valley. Situated along the western rim of the valley with sweeping views of Red Rock Canyon and the Spring Mountains, this 561-home enclave delivers the kind of modern desert luxury living that has made Summerlin the most sought-after master-planned community in Nevada.

Developed under the Howard Hughes Corporation's master plan, Ascension at The Peaks brings together several of the region's top production and semi-custom builders, including Toll Brothers, Shea Homes, and Taylor Morrison. The community features a curated selection of single-family homes ranging from approximately 2,500 to over 6,000 square feet, with architectural standards that emphasize contemporary desert design, clean lines, and indoor-outdoor living.

Homes in Ascension at The Peaks range from approximately $1 million for entry-level new construction to over $4 million for premium lots with unobstructed mountain views and upgraded custom finishes. The community's guard-gated entry and 24-hour security provide the privacy and exclusivity that high-net-worth buyers demand, while its location within Summerlin ensures access to world-class amenities, top-rated schools, and the 125+ shops and restaurants of Downtown Summerlin.

The Peaks village has rapidly emerged as one of Summerlin's most desirable addresses, attracting executives, professional athletes, and families seeking new-construction luxury without the multi-year timeline of a full custom build. Ascension's combination of builder variety, modern floor plans, and a premier Summerlin location makes it one of the most compelling luxury new-construction opportunities in the Las Vegas market.

name: Grand Park
overview: Grand Park is the newest and largest village within the Summerlin master-planned community, representing the next generation of desert living in the Las Vegas Valley. Spanning approximately 2,000 acres along the northwestern edge of Summerlin, Grand Park is being developed by the Howard Hughes Corporation as a self-contained village with its own parks, trails, schools, and commercial centers — while maintaining full integration with Summerlin's broader amenity network.

With 13 distinct sub-communities planned at full build-out, Grand Park offers a remarkable breadth of housing options rarely found within a single village. Entry-level homes from builders like Lennar and KB Home start in the $400,000 range, while guard-gated enclaves like Carlisle Peak and Glenrock deliver semi-custom luxury homes exceeding $2 million. This diversity makes Grand Park attractive to first-time buyers, move-up families, and luxury buyers alike.

Grand Park's location along the western valley rim provides many homesites with dramatic views of Red Rock Canyon and the Spring Mountains. The village is anchored by a planned 50-acre community park — one of the largest in Summerlin's history — that will include athletic fields, playgrounds, event lawns, and extensive trail connections to Summerlin's 250+ mile trail network.

Active construction from more than a dozen national and regional builders means inventory turns over rapidly, with new model homes and floor plans regularly introduced. Grand Park is expected to be one of the primary growth engines for Summerlin through the end of the decade, making it both a compelling lifestyle choice and a strong investment opportunity in one of Nevada's most proven real estate markets.

name: Peccole Ranch
overview: Peccole Ranch is one of the most established and centrally located master-planned communities in Las Vegas, encompassing 640 acres and more than 4,200 homes in 22 distinct neighborhoods. Developed by the Peccole family beginning in 1989, the community occupies a prime position in the western Las Vegas Valley, bordered by Charleston Boulevard to the south, Sahara Avenue to the north, and Durango Drive to the west.

What sets Peccole Ranch apart from other Las Vegas master plans is its remarkable range of housing options within a compact, mature community. The neighborhood portfolio spans from well-maintained single-family homes in the $400,000 range to the ultra-luxury guard-gated enclave of Queensridge, where estates regularly trade above $3 million. This diversity — combined with the community's central location — makes Peccole Ranch one of the most versatile residential choices in the valley.

The community is anchored by the award-winning Badlands Golf Club site (formerly a 27-hole championship course) and the iconic Queensridge Towers. Mature landscaping, wide streets, and established neighborhood character give Peccole Ranch an appeal that newer master plans cannot replicate. The surrounding commercial corridor along Sahara and Charleston provides walkable access to shopping, dining, and services.

Peccole Ranch's central location delivers drive times that are difficult to match elsewhere in Las Vegas — 15 minutes to the Strip, 10 minutes to Summerlin's shopping and dining, and easy freeway access via the 215 Beltway and US-95. For buyers who want an established community with mature trees, proven property values, and proximity to everything, Peccole Ranch remains one of the smartest choices in the market.

faqs.q: What is the price range for homes in Peccole Ranch?
faqs.a: Peccole Ranch offers homes from approximately $400,000 for entry-level single-family homes to over $3 million in the guard-gated Queensridge enclave. The community's 22 neighborhoods span virtually every price point.

faqs.q: How many neighborhoods are in Peccole Ranch?
faqs.a: Peccole Ranch includes 22 distinct neighborhoods across its 640 acres, ranging from starter homes to guard-gated luxury communities like Queensridge.

faqs.q: Is Peccole Ranch guard-gated?
faqs.a: Peccole Ranch itself is not guard-gated, but it contains Queensridge, a 987-home guard-gated community within its boundaries. Some individual neighborhoods within Peccole Ranch also have gated entry.

faqs.q: What ZIP codes cover Peccole Ranch?
faqs.a: Peccole Ranch spans ZIP codes 89117 and 89145, located in the western Las Vegas Valley.

faqs.q: Is Queensridge part of Peccole Ranch?

faqs.q: What happened to the Badlands Golf Club?
faqs.a: The Badlands Golf Club was a 27-hole championship course within Peccole Ranch that ceased operations. The site has been the subject of extensive development proposals and legal proceedings regarding potential residential redevelopment.

faqs.q: What schools serve Peccole Ranch?
faqs.a: Peccole Ranch is served by CCSD schools including Scherkenbach Elementary (7/10), Canarelli Middle School (7/10), and Palo Verde High School (8/10). The Meadows School (A+) is located adjacent to the community.

faqs.q: How old is Peccole Ranch?
faqs.a: Development of Peccole Ranch began in 1989, making it one of the more established master-planned communities in Las Vegas. The mature landscaping and proven property values are among its key selling points.

name: Queensridge
overview: Queensridge is one of the most prestigious guard-gated communities in the Las Vegas Valley, encompassing 987 luxury homes across approximately 300 acres within the Peccole Ranch master plan. Developed beginning in 1997, Queensridge quickly established itself as the premier address in the western valley, attracting executives, entrepreneurs, and high-net-worth individuals seeking estate-caliber living with unparalleled convenience.

The community is anchored by the iconic One Queensridge Place — twin 18-story luxury condominium towers that define the western Las Vegas skyline. Below the towers, the neighborhood features an impressive collection of custom and semi-custom single-family homes ranging from approximately 3,000 to over 10,000 square feet, many set on generous lots with mature landscaping, private pools, and sweeping views of the mountains and city lights.

Queensridge's guard-gated entry with 24-hour security staffing, combined with its meticulously maintained common areas and lush greenbelts, creates an atmosphere of exclusivity that rivals Summerlin's most expensive enclaves — at a more accessible price point. Homes range from approximately $800,000 for resale properties to over $5 million for premium estates and penthouse units in One Queensridge Place.

The community's central location is one of its greatest advantages. Queensridge sits just 15 minutes from the Las Vegas Strip, 10 minutes from Downtown Summerlin, and has immediate access to the 215 Beltway. For buyers who want guard-gated luxury without the drive times associated with more remote communities, Queensridge offers a rare combination of prestige, privacy, and proximity.

name: MacDonald Ranch
overview: MacDonald Ranch is one of the most expansive and diverse residential areas in Henderson, spanning approximately 3,200 acres along the eastern foothills of the McCullough Range. Developed by MacDonald Properties beginning in 1994, this master-planned area has grown to encompass more than 8,000 homes across multiple distinct communities, from active-adult neighborhoods to ultra-luxury guard-gated enclaves.

The MacDonald Ranch umbrella includes several of Henderson's most recognized communities: Sun City MacDonald Ranch (a Del Webb 55+ community), The Foothills at MacDonald Ranch (a guard-gated luxury enclave), Sunridge at MacDonald Ranch (a family-oriented neighborhood with golf views), and MacDonald Highlands (Henderson's premier luxury community with the DragonRidge Country Club). This diversity makes MacDonald Ranch a hub for virtually every buyer profile.

The area's elevated position along the Henderson foothills provides many homes with dramatic panoramic views of the Las Vegas Strip, the surrounding mountain ranges, and the desert landscape. Combined with Henderson's consistently top-ranked quality of life — including low crime, excellent schools, and abundant parks — MacDonald Ranch has become one of the most sought-after addresses in Southern Nevada.

Homes across the MacDonald Ranch area range from approximately $300,000 in Sun City MacDonald Ranch to over $15 million in MacDonald Highlands' most exclusive enclaves. This extraordinary breadth of housing options, combined with the area's natural beauty and proximity to Henderson's dining, shopping, and recreation, makes MacDonald Ranch a cornerstone of the Henderson real estate market.

highlights.body: Sun City MacDonald Ranch, The Foothills, Sunridge, and MacDonald Highlands — each community offers a distinct lifestyle within the broader MacDonald Ranch area.

highlights.body: Elevated position along the McCullough Range provides panoramic views of the Las Vegas Strip and surrounding desert from hundreds of homesites.

highlights.body: The Foothills and MacDonald Highlands both offer guard-gated living with custom and semi-custom homes from $1 million to $15 million+.

highlights.title: Proven Value Appreciation
highlights.body: 30+ years of development history with strong, consistent appreciation. One of Henderson's most established and desirable residential areas.

faqs.a: The MacDonald Ranch area includes Sun City MacDonald Ranch (55+), The Foothills at MacDonald Ranch (guard-gated luxury), Sunridge at MacDonald Ranch (family), and MacDonald Highlands (ultra-luxury with DragonRidge Country Club).

faqs.q: Is MacDonald Ranch in Henderson or Las Vegas?
faqs.a: MacDonald Ranch is located in Henderson, Nevada — the second-largest city in the Las Vegas metro area. Henderson is consistently ranked among the safest and most livable cities in America.

faqs.q: What are guard-gated options in MacDonald Ranch?
faqs.a: MacDonald Highlands and The Foothills at MacDonald Ranch are both guard-gated communities. MacDonald Highlands' Dragon Rock enclave is double guard-gated for maximum privacy and exclusivity.

name: Four Seasons Private Residences
overview: Four Seasons Private Residences Las Vegas is the most anticipated ultra-luxury residential development in the history of the Las Vegas Valley. Located within the guard-gated MacDonald Highlands community in Henderson, this 171-unit branded residence project brings the legendary Four Seasons hospitality brand to the residential market for the first time in Nevada, offering a level of service and luxury that is unprecedented in the region.

The project features a curated collection of residences ranging from approximately $3.67 million for entry-level units to over $28.95 million for the most exclusive penthouses and estate residences. Each home is designed with the Four Seasons standard of excellence, featuring premium finishes, expansive floor plans, and floor-to-ceiling glass that frames panoramic views of the Las Vegas Strip, the McCullough Range, and the desert landscape stretching to the horizon.

Residents of Four Seasons Private Residences enjoy a full suite of Four Seasons services, including 24-hour concierge, valet parking, housekeeping, in-residence dining, spa services, and access to resort-caliber amenities including infinity pools, a state-of-the-art fitness center, private dining rooms, and curated social programming. The development also benefits from its location within MacDonald Highlands, providing access to DragonRidge Country Club's Tom Fazio-designed championship golf course.

With approximately 75% of units reported sold during pre-construction, Four Seasons Private Residences has validated Las Vegas as a legitimate destination for branded ultra-luxury living. The project is expected to begin deliveries in 2027, establishing a new ceiling for residential real estate pricing in the Las Vegas metro area and attracting an international clientele of high-net-worth individuals seeking a primary residence, second home, or investment property.

highlights.body: Full Four Seasons hospitality including 24-hour concierge, in-residence dining, housekeeping, valet, and spa services — the first branded residences of this caliber in Nevada.

highlights.body: Residences from $3.67 million to over $28.95 million, establishing a new pricing benchmark for the Las Vegas metro luxury market.

faqs.a: Monthly fees at Four Seasons Private Residences are estimated between $2,000 and $5,000+ per month, reflecting the full-service Four Seasons hospitality model including concierge, security, housekeeping options, and resort amenities.

faqs.q: Can I play golf at DragonRidge as a Four Seasons resident?
faqs.a: Four Seasons Private Residences is located within MacDonald Highlands, which is home to DragonRidge Country Club. Membership at DragonRidge is separate and available to residents on a fee basis.

faqs.q: Is this a hotel or private residences?
faqs.a: Four Seasons Private Residences are privately owned homes — not hotel units. Owners receive Four Seasons-branded services and amenities, but the residences are full-time homes with private ownership, not timeshares or hotel-condo units.

name: Heartland at Tule Springs
overview: Heartland at Tule Springs is one of the most dynamic new-construction master-planned communities in the Las Vegas Valley, located in the rapidly growing northern corridor of North Las Vegas. Ranked among the top 50 best-selling master-planned communities nationally, Heartland has attracted thousands of buyers with its combination of brand-new homes, competitive pricing, modern amenities, and proximity to the Tule Springs Fossil Beds National Monument.

The community features homes from several of the nation's top production builders, including Lennar, KB Home, Century Communities, and Richmond American Homes. Floor plans range from compact single-family homes around 1,400 square feet to spacious two-story residences exceeding 3,000 square feet, with pricing that makes homeownership accessible to first-time buyers, young families, and investors.

Heartland's master plan includes an extensive network of parks, trails, and community amenities, with planned commercial centers to serve the growing population. The adjacent Tule Springs Fossil Beds National Monument — a 22,650-acre preserve protecting Ice Age fossils — provides a unique natural amenity that no other Las Vegas community can match. Residents also benefit from proximity to the VA Medical Center and the planned developments along the 215 northern beltway extension.

North Las Vegas has emerged as one of the fastest-growing cities in the United States, and Heartland at Tule Springs is at the center of that growth. For buyers seeking new-construction homes at price points significantly below Summerlin, Henderson, or the southwest valley, Heartland represents one of the strongest value propositions in the Las Vegas metro area.

name: Canyon Gate
overview: Canyon Gate is one of the Las Vegas Valley's most established guard-gated golf course communities, encompassing 330 acres and over 800 homes in a premier location along the western edge of the valley. Developed in 1989, Canyon Gate was one of the first luxury guard-gated communities in Las Vegas and has maintained its reputation as one of the most desirable residential addresses for golf enthusiasts and families seeking privacy, security, and timeless elegance.

The community is anchored by the Canyon Gate Country Club, an 18-hole championship golf course designed by Ted Robinson Sr., known for its lush fairways, strategic water features, and mature landscaping. The country club offers dining, a pro shop, tennis courts, a swimming pool, and a full calendar of social events, creating a true country club lifestyle within the community's secure perimeter.

Homes in Canyon Gate range from approximately $700,000 for resale properties to over $2 million for premium golf course lots and renovated custom estates. The architectural styles span Mediterranean, Spanish Colonial, and Contemporary designs on generous lots with mature landscaping. Many homes back directly onto the golf course, offering fairway views and a sense of openness rarely found in a community so centrally located.

Canyon Gate's central position — 15 minutes to the Strip, 10 minutes to Summerlin, and immediate access to major freeways — makes it one of the most conveniently located guard-gated golf communities in the valley. For buyers who want the country club lifestyle without the remote drive times of newer luxury communities, Canyon Gate delivers a compelling combination of location, privacy, and established community character.

highlights.body: Canyon Gate Country Club features an 18-hole Ted Robinson Sr.-designed course with water features, mature trees, and meticulously maintained fairways.

highlights.title: Guard-Gated Security
highlights.body: 24-hour staffed guard gate with comprehensive security patrols. One of Las Vegas' most established guard-gated communities since 1989.

highlights.title: Country Club Lifestyle
highlights.body: Full country club amenities including dining, tennis courts, swimming pool, pro shop, and an active social calendar for members.

highlights.body: 15 minutes to the Strip, 10 minutes to Summerlin, and immediate freeway access. One of the most centrally located golf communities in Las Vegas.

highlights.body: Generous lots with decades-old landscaping, many backing directly onto golf course fairways with unobstructed views.

highlights.title: Proven Property Values
highlights.body: 35+ years of established property values with consistent appreciation. Canyon Gate homes hold their value through every market cycle.

name: Cello Tower
overview: Cello Tower is the first new luxury high-rise condominium development to break ground in Downtown Las Vegas in over 15 years, signaling a new era of urban luxury living in the heart of the city. Rising above the intersection of Symphony Park and the Arts District, this 240-unit tower is bringing a level of residential sophistication to downtown that was previously only available on the Las Vegas Strip or in suburban guard-gated communities.

The project features residences ranging from approximately $700,000 for studio and one-bedroom units to over $4.5 million for the tower's most exclusive penthouses. Every unit is designed with floor-to-ceiling glass, premium finishes, and smart home technology. The building's architecture makes a deliberate statement — a modern glass tower that adds to the evolving downtown skyline while complementing the nearby Smith Center for the Performing Arts and the World Market Center.

Residents of Cello Tower will have access to resort-caliber amenities including a rooftop infinity pool and lounge, state-of-the-art fitness center, private dining and entertainment spaces, co-working facilities, concierge services, and secure parking. The building's ground-floor retail component will bring curated dining and shopping directly to residents' doorsteps.

Cello Tower's location in Downtown Las Vegas positions residents at the epicenter of the city's cultural renaissance. The Arts District, Fremont East, Symphony Park, and the forthcoming developments along the UNLV Medical District corridor are transforming downtown into a vibrant urban neighborhood. For buyers seeking a walkable, culturally rich lifestyle that is distinctly different from suburban Las Vegas, Cello Tower represents the most compelling opportunity in the market.

highlights.body: The first new luxury high-rise to break ground in Downtown Las Vegas in over 15 years, marking a milestone in the city's urban residential renaissance.

highlights.body: Resort-style rooftop amenity deck with infinity pool, lounge areas, and panoramic views of the Strip, mountains, and downtown skyline.

highlights.body: Every residence features floor-to-ceiling glass walls with panoramic views, premium finishes, and integrated smart home technology.

highlights.body: Steps from the Arts District, Fremont East, Smith Center for the Performing Arts, and downtown's growing dining and entertainment scene.

highlights.body: Full-service concierge, secure parking, fitness center, co-working spaces, private dining rooms, and ground-floor retail.

highlights.body: Downtown Las Vegas is undergoing a multi-billion-dollar transformation. Cello Tower positions buyers at the center of one of the fastest-appreciating urban corridors in the West.

name: Astra at La Madre Peaks
overview: Astra at La Madre Peaks is the newest ultra-luxury custom lot community within Summerlin, offering 167 premium homesites in one of the most elevated and scenic positions in the entire Las Vegas Valley. Located within the La Madre Peaks village along Summerlin's western rim, Astra backs directly against the Spring Mountains and provides panoramic views of Red Rock Canyon, the Las Vegas Strip, and the surrounding desert landscape.

Unlike the production and semi-custom communities that comprise most of Summerlin's newer villages, Astra is exclusively a custom lot community — meaning buyers purchase a finished lot and select their own architect and builder to design a one-of-a-kind estate. Lots start at approximately $2 million, with finished homes expected to range from $4 million to well over $10 million depending on size, views, and custom specifications.

The community is being developed by the Howard Hughes Corporation with the same attention to architectural standards and community design that defined The Ridges and The Summit Club. Guard-gated entry with 24-hour security, underground utilities, and strict design guidelines ensure a cohesive ultra-luxury aesthetic while allowing buyers the freedom of full custom design. Approved builders include some of the valley's most respected luxury home builders.

Astra at La Madre Peaks represents the next evolution of Summerlin's luxury real estate offering — a community designed for buyers who want the security and infrastructure of a master-planned community with the creative freedom of a full custom build. For high-net-worth individuals seeking to build their dream estate in the most desirable location in Las Vegas, Astra is the definitive opportunity.

highlights.body: Positioned within La Madre Peaks, one of the most elevated villages in Summerlin, offering panoramic views of Red Rock Canyon, the Strip, and the Spring Mountains.

highlights.body: Full access to Summerlin's 250+ miles of trails, 150+ parks, Downtown Summerlin, and top-rated schools. The ultimate Summerlin lifestyle.

name: The Estates at Southern Highlands
overview: The Estates at Southern Highlands is the premier residential enclave within the larger Southern Highlands master-planned community. Tucked behind its own guard gate within the already guard-gated Southern Highlands development, The Estates offers double-gated privacy and some of the most expansive custom home lots in the Las Vegas Valley. Homes here sit on half-acre to full-acre lots, many with direct frontage along the Southern Highlands Golf Club, a private championship course originally designed by Robert Trent Jones Jr. and later redesigned by Jack Nicklaus.

Custom estates in The Estates range from approximately $2 million to over $10 million, with the most coveted properties occupying elevated positions with unobstructed mountain and golf course views. The architectural character blends desert contemporary, Mediterranean, and transitional styles, with strict CC&Rs maintaining a cohesive and visually impressive streetscape. Homes typically range from 5,000 to 12,000+ square feet, with many featuring resort-style pools, motor courts, and guest casitas.

The Estates benefits from its position within Southern Highlands, one of Las Vegas's most consistently prestigious addresses. The private golf club, 24-hour security, and the community's proximity to Harry Reid International Airport (approximately 15 minutes via I-15) make it a natural choice for executives, business owners, and high-net-worth individuals who travel frequently. The adjacent Southern Highlands Golf Club is the social anchor of the community, offering dining, fitness, and event facilities reserved for members.

For buyers seeking the highest tier of Las Vegas living without the Summerlin price premium, The Estates at Southern Highlands delivers custom estate living, private golf, and double guard-gated security at price points that represent genuine value compared to comparable communities like The Ridges or Ascaya.

highlights.title: Double Guard-Gated Security
highlights.body: Residents pass through the Southern Highlands community guard gate and then a second private guard gate to reach The Estates. Two layers of 24-hour staffed security provide unmatched privacy.

highlights.title: Jack Nicklaus Golf Frontage
highlights.body: Many estate lots sit directly along the Southern Highlands Golf Club, a Jack Nicklaus-redesigned private championship course consistently ranked among the top courses in Nevada.

highlights.title: Custom Half-Acre+ Lots
highlights.body: The Estates features some of the largest residential lots in Southern Highlands, with half-acre to full-acre parcels allowing truly custom home construction with resort-style outdoor living.

highlights.title: 15 Minutes to Airport
highlights.body: Harry Reid International Airport is approximately 15 minutes south via I-15 — the shortest luxury-community-to-airport commute in Las Vegas, ideal for frequent business travelers.

highlights.title: Mountain & Golf Views
highlights.body: Elevated estate lots offer panoramic views of the Spring Mountains to the west and sweeping fairway views of the Nicklaus course. Sunset views are among the best in the south valley.

highlights.title: Strong Appreciation History
highlights.body: The Estates has demonstrated consistent appreciation through market cycles, benefiting from the scarcity of large custom lots within a guard-gated golf community in Las Vegas.

faqs.q: What is the price range for homes in The Estates at Southern Highlands?
faqs.a: Custom estates in The Estates range from approximately $2 million for older resale homes to over $10 million for new-build custom estates on premium golf course lots with panoramic mountain views.

faqs.q: Is The Estates at Southern Highlands guard-gated?
faqs.a: Yes — The Estates is double guard-gated. Residents pass through the main Southern Highlands community guard gate and then a second private guard gate to enter The Estates. Both gates are staffed 24 hours a day.

faqs.q: What ZIP code is The Estates at Southern Highlands in?
faqs.a: The Estates at Southern Highlands is located in ZIP code 89141 in the southernmost area of Las Vegas, Nevada.

faqs.q: What golf course is near The Estates?
faqs.a: The Estates sits directly along the Southern Highlands Golf Club, a private championship course redesigned by Jack Nicklaus. Many estate lots have direct golf course frontage. Membership is by application and is not automatically included with home purchase.

faqs.q: How does The Estates compare to The Ridges in Summerlin?
faqs.a: Both are ultra-luxury guard-gated communities with golf course frontage. The Ridges is anchored by Bear's Best (Nicklaus design) and backs against Red Rock Canyon, while The Estates fronts the Southern Highlands Golf Club and is 15 minutes from the airport. The Ridges typically commands higher per-square-foot prices, but The Estates offers larger lots and more competitive pricing for comparable square footage.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees in The Estates?
faqs.a: HOA fees in The Estates typically range from $350 to $900 per month, which includes the Southern Highlands master association fee plus The Estates sub-association fee. Fees cover guard gate staffing, security patrols, common area maintenance, and landscaping of common areas.

faqs.q: How close is The Estates to the airport?
faqs.a: Harry Reid International Airport is approximately 15 minutes from The Estates via I-15, making it the closest ultra-luxury community to the airport in Las Vegas. This proximity is a primary draw for executives and frequent travelers.

faqs.q: Can I build a custom home in The Estates?
faqs.a: Yes — The Estates permits custom home construction on approved lots. Architectural plans must be reviewed and approved by the HOA architectural review committee to ensure compliance with community design standards. Several luxury builders, including Sun West Custom Homes, have built in The Estates.

name: Olympia Ridge
overview: Olympia Ridge is an ultra-luxury guard-gated enclave within Southern Highlands, positioned along the most coveted fairways of the Southern Highlands Golf Club. Developed by the Olympia Group, the community was conceived as a premier residential address for buyers who demand golf course living, privacy, and architectural distinction — without the Summerlin price premium that communities like The Ridges command.

Homes in Olympia Ridge range from approximately $1.5 million for resale semi-custom residences to over $5 million for newer custom estates on premium golf course lots. The architectural character is predominantly Mediterranean and Tuscan-inspired, though newer builds have introduced contemporary desert and transitional styles. Homes typically range from 4,000 to 10,000+ square feet, with many featuring expansive outdoor living spaces, infinity pools, and covered loggias overlooking the Jack Nicklaus-redesigned championship course.

The community's guard gate provides an additional layer of security beyond the Southern Highlands master community gates, creating a double-gated environment that appeals to executives, entertainers, and high-net-worth families. The proximity to the Southern Highlands Golf Club is Olympia Ridge's defining advantage — many homes have direct fairway views, and the clubhouse, dining facilities, and practice areas are within a short cart ride or walk.

Location is another significant draw. Harry Reid International Airport is approximately 15 minutes away, the Las Vegas Strip is 20 minutes north via I-15, and Henderson's retail and dining corridor along St. Rose Parkway is 15 minutes east. For luxury buyers who want private golf community living with genuine convenience, Olympia Ridge delivers at price points that represent strong value relative to comparable Summerlin guard-gated addresses.

highlights.title: Nicklaus Golf Club Frontage
highlights.body: Direct frontage along the Southern Highlands Golf Club, a private championship course redesigned by Jack Nicklaus. Fairway and mountain views from nearly every estate.

highlights.title: Double Guard-Gated Privacy
highlights.body: Protected by its own guard gate within the already guard-gated Southern Highlands community. Two layers of 24-hour staffed security for maximum privacy.

highlights.title: Semi-Custom & Custom Estates
highlights.body: Homes range from 4,000 to 10,000+ square feet with premium finishes, resort-style pools, and expansive outdoor living. Both resale and custom build opportunities.

highlights.title: 15-Minute Airport Commute
highlights.body: One of the closest luxury communities to Harry Reid International Airport — a critical advantage for executives and frequent business travelers.

highlights.title: Mountain & Valley Views
highlights.body: Elevated positions within Olympia Ridge offer panoramic views of the Spring Mountains, the Las Vegas Strip skyline, and the manicured fairways of the golf course.

highlights.title: Strong Value Proposition
highlights.body: Comparable quality to Summerlin's ultra-luxury communities at notably lower per-square-foot prices. One of the best value plays in Las Vegas luxury real estate.

faqs.q: What is the price range for homes in Olympia Ridge?
faqs.a: Homes in Olympia Ridge range from approximately $1.5 million for resale semi-custom residences to over $5 million for newer custom estates on premium golf course lots with panoramic mountain and fairway views.

faqs.q: Is Olympia Ridge guard-gated?
faqs.a: Yes — Olympia Ridge is double guard-gated. The community has its own 24-hour staffed guard gate within the larger Southern Highlands guard-gated master plan, providing two layers of security.

faqs.q: What golf course is in Olympia Ridge?
faqs.a: Olympia Ridge sits along the Southern Highlands Golf Club, a private championship course redesigned by Jack Nicklaus. Many homes have direct fairway frontage. Membership is by application and not included with home purchase.

faqs.q: How does Olympia Ridge compare to The Ridges?
faqs.a: Both are ultra-luxury guard-gated golf communities. The Ridges (Summerlin) backs against Red Rock Canyon and commands higher per-square-foot prices. Olympia Ridge offers comparable quality with larger lots and lower prices per square foot, plus a 15-minute airport commute versus 30+ minutes from The Ridges.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees in Olympia Ridge?
faqs.a: HOA fees in Olympia Ridge typically range from $300 to $800 per month, which includes the Southern Highlands master association fee plus the Olympia Ridge sub-association fee. Fees cover guard gate staffing, security patrols, and common area maintenance.

faqs.q: What ZIP code is Olympia Ridge in?
faqs.a: Olympia Ridge is located in ZIP code 89141 in the Southern Highlands area of Las Vegas, Nevada.

faqs.q: Is Olympia Ridge a good investment?
faqs.a: Olympia Ridge benefits from the same fundamentals that make all of Southern Highlands attractive — guard-gated prestige, private golf club anchoring, limited lot inventory, and consistent demand from executives and high-net-worth buyers. The community has demonstrated strong appreciation and holds value well through market cycles.

faqs.q: What is Olympia Ridge Estates?
faqs.a: Olympia Ridge Estates is the most exclusive section within Olympia Ridge, featuring the largest custom lots (half-acre+) with the most direct golf course views and the highest price points, typically starting above $3 million.

name: Tuscan Cliffs
overview: Tuscan Cliffs is one of the most popular guard-gated enclaves within Southern Highlands, offering Mediterranean and Tuscan-inspired luxury homes at price points more accessible than the community's ultra-luxury addresses. The community sits on elevated terrain within Southern Highlands, providing many homes with panoramic views of the Las Vegas Valley, the Spring Mountains, and the fairways of the adjacent Southern Highlands Golf Club.

Homes in Tuscan Cliffs range from approximately $800,000 for resale homes to over $3 million for premium golf course and ridgeline properties. The architectural character is distinctly Mediterranean — stucco exteriors, clay tile roofs, arched entryways, and stone accents create a cohesive Tuscan aesthetic throughout the community. Homes typically range from 3,000 to 7,000 square feet, with many featuring courtyard entries, wine rooms, and expansive covered patios.

The guard gate at Tuscan Cliffs provides an additional layer of security within the already guard-gated Southern Highlands master plan. This double-gated configuration, combined with the community's elevated position and manicured streetscapes, creates a sense of exclusivity that attracts professionals, executives, and families relocating from coastal California markets where comparable quality would command significantly higher prices.

Tuscan Cliffs occupies a strategic position in the Southern Highlands pricing hierarchy — more attainable than The Estates or Olympia Ridge, but with the same double guard-gated security, golf club proximity, and airport convenience that define Southern Highlands' premium enclaves. For buyers seeking guard-gated luxury in the south valley without the $2M+ entry point of the top-tier communities, Tuscan Cliffs represents one of the strongest value propositions in Las Vegas.

highlights.body: Cohesive Tuscan-inspired design throughout — stucco facades, clay tile roofs, stone accents, and courtyard entries create a distinguished Mediterranean village aesthetic.

highlights.body: Zoned for strong CCSD schools including Liberty High School. Private options including Faith Lutheran and Bishop Gorman are within 15 minutes.

highlights.body: Guard-gated luxury living starting around $800K — approximately 40-60% less than comparable quality in Summerlin's ultra-luxury communities like The Ridges.

name: Carlisle Peak
overview: Carlisle Peak is one of Summerlin's newest guard-gated luxury communities, located within the Grand Park village in Summerlin West. Developed by Tri Pointe Homes, Carlisle Peak delivers new-construction luxury in a guard-gated setting at price points that represent a compelling alternative to established ultra-luxury communities like The Ridges. The community began sales in 2023 and features homes designed specifically for the Las Vegas luxury buyer — open floor plans, indoor-outdoor living, and dramatic mountain views of the Spring Mountains and Red Rock Canyon escarpment.

Homes in Carlisle Peak start at approximately $1.55 million and extend beyond $2 million for premium lots with upgraded finishes. Floor plans range from approximately 3,500 to 5,200+ square feet, featuring 4–6 bedrooms, gourmet kitchens, multi-car garages, and the contemporary desert architecture that defines Summerlin's newest construction. Tri Pointe is known for its attention to design and finish quality, and Carlisle Peak reflects that reputation.

Grand Park is Summerlin's most actively developing village, positioned in the Summerlin West Association. This means Carlisle Peak buyers benefit from the newest infrastructure, the most modern community amenities, and direct proximity to the trail systems that connect to Red Rock Canyon. The village is also adjacent to the future Grand Park community park, which will be one of Summerlin's largest when completed.

The guard-gated entry at Carlisle Peak sets it apart from the majority of Grand Park's neighborhoods, which are gated but not guard-gated. For luxury buyers who want new construction with the security of a staffed guard gate, proximity to Red Rock Canyon, and the Summerlin address, Carlisle Peak is one of a very small number of current options.

highlights.body: Brand-new homes by Tri Pointe Homes with the latest designs, finishes, and smart home technology. No renovation or deferred maintenance — move-in ready luxury.

highlights.body: One of the few guard-gated communities within Grand Park village. 24-hour staffed guard gate provides a level of security uncommon in new Summerlin construction.

highlights.body: Located in Summerlin West, approximately 5–10 minutes from Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area. Direct trail connectivity through the Summerlin trail system.

highlights.body: Tri Pointe Homes is known for premium design and construction quality. Floor plans range from 3,500 to 5,200+ square feet with contemporary desert architecture.

highlights.body: Adjacent to Grand Park's future community park — one of Summerlin's largest. Access to the broader Summerlin trail system and community recreation facilities.

highlights.title: Scarcity-Protected Value
highlights.body: Guard-gated new construction in Summerlin is extremely limited. Carlisle Peak occupies a narrow market niche with strong long-term value protection from both scarcity and the Summerlin brand.

name: The Hills South
overview: The Hills South is one of Summerlin's most prestigious villages, distinguished by the concentration of guard-gated enclaves within its borders. Located in the Summerlin South Association, The Hills South encompasses four separate guard-gated communities — Country Club Hills, Eagle Hills, Corte Bella, and Tournament Hills — each with its own character, price range, and security infrastructure. This makes The Hills South one of the most desirable villages in all of Summerlin for buyers who prioritize privacy and exclusivity.

Homes in The Hills South range from approximately $600,000 in the non-gated neighborhoods to over $3 million in the guard-gated enclaves, with Country Club Hills and Tournament Hills commanding the highest premiums due to their golf course proximity and larger estate lots. The village benefits from its central Summerlin South location — close to Downtown Summerlin, TPC Summerlin, and the Red Rock Country Club, while remaining a short drive from Red Rock Canyon and the western mountain trails.

The architectural character in The Hills South evolved over multiple development phases beginning in the late 1990s. The non-gated neighborhoods feature the classic Summerlin aesthetic — Mediterranean and transitional styles with mature landscaping. The guard-gated enclaves are more varied, with Country Club Hills featuring larger custom and semi-custom estates, and Tournament Hills offering golf course frontage along the TPC Summerlin Championship Course.

For buyers evaluating Summerlin villages, The Hills South represents a sweet spot — it offers genuine guard-gated luxury at price points below The Ridges or The Peaks, while providing the mature landscaping, established neighborhood character, and central location that newer villages like Grand Park and Stonebridge cannot yet match. The presence of four separate guard-gated options within one village also gives buyers unusual flexibility in finding the right fit.

highlights.body: Country Club Hills, Eagle Hills, Corte Bella, and Tournament Hills — four distinct guard-gated communities within one village, each with its own character and price range.

highlights.body: Tournament Hills and Country Club Hills sit adjacent to TPC Summerlin, the PGA Tour championship course that hosts the annual Shriners Children's Open.

highlights.body: Ideal central positioning within Summerlin South — close to Downtown Summerlin, Red Rock Country Club, and major trail access without being on the outer perimeter.

highlights.title: Mature Landscaping
highlights.body: Established neighborhoods with 20+ years of mature landscaping. Tree-lined streets and established desert gardens create a neighborhood character that newer communities cannot replicate.

highlights.title: Excellent School Zoning
highlights.body: Zoned for top-performing CCSD schools including Sig Rogich Middle and Palo Verde High School. Private schools like The Meadows School are minutes away.

highlights.title: Broad Price Range
highlights.body: From $600K in non-gated neighborhoods to $3M+ in the guard-gated enclaves. One of the few Summerlin villages that serves both the move-up and luxury buyer.

name: Siena
overview: Siena is Summerlin's premier guard-gated 55+ active adult community, located in the Summerlin South Association. Developed by Shea Homes beginning in 2000, Siena spans approximately 270 acres and contains over 1,200 homes, making it one of the largest and most established age-qualified communities in the Las Vegas Valley. The community is anchored by a 30,000-square-foot private clubhouse — the Siena Clubhouse — which serves as the social, fitness, and recreational hub for residents.

Homes in Siena range from approximately $400,000 for smaller single-story patio homes to over $900,000 for the larger premium models in Sun Colony at Siena, the community's most upscale section. The architectural style is uniformly single-story desert contemporary and Mediterranean, with low-maintenance landscaping and open floor plans designed for active adult living. Most homes feature 1,500 to 3,200 square feet with 2–3 bedrooms, gourmet kitchens, and covered patios.

The Siena Clubhouse is the community's crown jewel — a resort-caliber facility with an indoor pool, outdoor resort pool, state-of-the-art fitness center, tennis courts, pickleball courts, card rooms, arts and crafts studios, a demonstration kitchen, and a full calendar of organized social activities, clubs, and events. The HOA maintains all front-yard landscaping, exterior painting, and common areas, allowing residents to focus on the lifestyle rather than home maintenance.

Siena's guard-gated entry distinguishes it from other 55+ communities in the valley, including the much larger Sun City Summerlin (which is not guard-gated). For active adults who want the Summerlin address, the security of a staffed guard gate, and a vibrant social infrastructure without the scale of a Sun City, Siena is the clear first choice.

highlights.title: Guard-Gated 55+ Security
highlights.body: 24-hour staffed guard gate provides genuine security and privacy — a feature that distinguishes Siena from larger 55+ communities like Sun City Summerlin which rely on access codes only.

highlights.title: 30,000 SF Clubhouse
highlights.body: The Siena Clubhouse features indoor and outdoor pools, state-of-the-art fitness center, tennis and pickleball courts, card rooms, art studios, and a full social calendar.

highlights.title: Low-Maintenance Living
