name: Las Vegas Communities
overview: The Las Vegas Valley is home to over 250 distinct residential communities spanning master-planned neighborhoods, guard-gated enclaves, golf course communities, 55+ active adult villages, high-rise condominiums, and vintage historic districts. From new construction in the booming northwest corridor to established luxury in Summerlin and Henderson, every buyer can find the right community to match their lifestyle, budget, and priorities.

Master-planned communities like Summerlin, Southern Highlands, Anthem, and Cadence offer resort-style amenities, parks, trails, and top-rated schools within carefully designed neighborhoods. Guard-gated communities such as The Ridges, MacDonald Highlands, Ascaya, Spanish Trail, and Anthem Country Club provide the highest levels of privacy, security, and exclusivity in the valley.

For active adults 55 and older, communities like Sun City Summerlin, Sun City Anthem, and Trilogy at Summerlin deliver world-class recreation centers, championship golf, and social calendars with hundreds of clubs and organizations. Luxury high-rise living along the Strip corridor — Veer Towers, Waldorf Astoria, Turnberry Place — offers a completely different lifestyle with concierge services, resort pools, and panoramic skyline views.

My team at Nevada Real Estate Group has deep expertise in every corner of the valley. We've closed transactions in over 100 Las Vegas communities, and we know the schools, the builders, the HOA landscapes, and the investment trajectories that matter to buyers. Use this page to explore the valley's communities, or call us directly — we'll match you with the neighborhoods that fit your life.

highlights.title: 250+ Communities
highlights.body: The Las Vegas Valley contains over 250 distinct residential communities across Las Vegas, Henderson, North Las Vegas, Summerlin, and Boulder City.

highlights.title: Master-Planned Living
highlights.body: Summerlin, Southern Highlands, Anthem, Cadence, Inspirada, and Skye Canyon are among the top-selling master-planned communities in the United States.

highlights.title: Guard-Gated Luxury
highlights.body: Over 50 guard-gated communities offer 24-hour staffed security, exclusive amenities, and Nevada's most prestigious addresses.

highlights.title: 55+ Active Adult
highlights.body: Del Webb, Trilogy, and other 55+ communities provide resort-style retirement living with golf, recreation centers, and vibrant social programs.

highlights.title: New Construction
highlights.body: Active new home construction across the valley from builders like Toll Brothers, Lennar, KB Home, Taylor Morrison, and Pulte. Prices from the $300Ks to $5M+.

highlights.title: High-Rise Condos
highlights.body: 20+ high-rise towers on or near the Strip offer lock-and-leave luxury, concierge living, and panoramic Las Vegas skyline views.

neighborhoods.name: Summerlin
neighborhoods.desc: Nevada's premier master-planned community spanning 22,500 acres with 20+ villages, guard-gated enclaves, and world-class amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Henderson
neighborhoods.desc: America's safest large city. Home to Anthem, Green Valley, MacDonald Highlands, Seven Hills, Lake Las Vegas, Inspirada, and Cadence.

neighborhoods.name: Southern Highlands
neighborhoods.desc: Premier south-valley master plan with guard-gated estates, Jack Nicklaus golf, and sweeping mountain views.

neighborhoods.name: Northwest Las Vegas
neighborhoods.desc: Skye Canyon, Centennial Hills, Providence, and Lone Mountain in the valley's fastest-growing family corridor.

neighborhoods.name: North Las Vegas
neighborhoods.desc: Aliante, Tule Springs, and Ardiente offer new construction and strong value in a rapidly developing area.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown & Historic
neighborhoods.desc: The Arts District, Scotch 80s, Rancho Circle, and mid-century neighborhoods offer character and walkability in the city's revitalized core.

faqs.q: How many communities are in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The Las Vegas Valley contains over 250 distinct residential communities across Las Vegas, Henderson, North Las Vegas, Summerlin, and Boulder City. These range from affordable starter neighborhoods to ultra-luxury guard-gated enclaves.

faqs.q: What is the best community in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The best community depends on your priorities. Summerlin is the most popular master-planned community. MacDonald Highlands and The Ridges offer ultra-luxury. Sun City Anthem is the top 55+ community. Skye Canyon leads new construction. We help match buyers to the right community based on lifestyle, budget, and preferences.

faqs.q: What are the safest communities in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Henderson is consistently ranked among America's safest large cities. Guard-gated communities like The Ridges, Ascaya, MacDonald Highlands, Southern Highlands, and Anthem Country Club provide the highest levels of security in the valley.

faqs.q: Which Las Vegas communities have the best schools?
faqs.a: Summerlin, Henderson (Anthem, Green Valley, Inspirada), and the northwest corridor (Skye Canyon, Centennial Hills) are known for top-rated public schools. Private schools like The Meadows, Bishop Gorman, and Faith Lutheran serve the entire valley.

faqs.q: What are the most affordable communities in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: North Las Vegas communities like Aliante and Tule Springs, plus Spring Valley, Enterprise, and parts of Henderson (Silverado Ranch, Whitney Ranch) offer homes from the low $300Ks. These areas provide excellent value with good amenities and accessibility.

faqs.q: Which Las Vegas communities are best for retirees?
faqs.a: Sun City Summerlin, Sun City Anthem, Trilogy at Summerlin, Siena in Summerlin, and Ardiente in North Las Vegas are the valley's premier 55+ active adult communities with recreation centers, golf, and vibrant social programs.

name: Guard-Gated Communities
overview: Las Vegas is home to over 50 guard-gated residential communities, making it one of the most secure luxury real estate markets in the United States. From ultra-exclusive enclaves like The Summit Club and Ascaya to established golf communities like Spanish Trail and Red Rock Country Club, guard-gated living in Las Vegas offers 24-hour staffed security, controlled access, and an elevated standard of privacy that attracts high-net-worth individuals, executives, professional athletes, and entertainers.

Guard-gated communities in the Las Vegas Valley span every price point from approximately $400,000 at entry-level gated communities like Los Prados and Silverstone Ranch to over $20 million at The Ridges in Summerlin, MacDonald Highlands in Henderson, and the invitation-only Summit Club. Many of the valley's guard-gated communities also feature championship golf courses, private clubhouses, resort-style pools, and fitness centers exclusively for residents.

Summerlin leads the valley with the most guard-gated sub-communities, including The Ridges, Tournament Hills, Bellacere, and the newly-built enclaves in The Peaks and Grand Park villages. Henderson offers Ascaya, MacDonald Highlands, Anthem Country Club, Seven Hills, and The Fountains. The southwest valley features Southern Highlands, Spanish Trail, and Rhodes Ranch. Central Las Vegas adds Canyon Gate Country Club and Las Vegas Country Club.

I've helped hundreds of buyers navigate the guard-gated market in Las Vegas. Each community has a different personality — some are golf-centric, some are purely residential, some skew toward families while others cater to empty-nesters. My team knows the HOA rules, the lot premiums, the security protocols, and the resale dynamics of every guard-gated community in the valley. Let us match you with the right one.

highlights.title: 50+ Guard-Gated Communities
highlights.body: The Las Vegas Valley has over 50 guard-gated communities spread across Summerlin, Henderson, Southern Highlands, and central Las Vegas — more than nearly any metro in the country.

highlights.title: 24-Hour Staffed Security
highlights.body: Guard-gated communities feature staffed guard gates with controlled vehicular access, visitor management, security patrols, and surveillance systems for maximum privacy.

highlights.title: Championship Golf
highlights.body: Many guard-gated communities feature private or semi-private golf courses including Bear's Best, DragonRidge, Southern Highlands Golf Club, Spanish Trail, and Canyon Gate.

highlights.title: Ultra-Luxury Estates
highlights.body: Custom estates in The Ridges, Ascaya, The Summit Club, and MacDonald Highlands rank among the most valuable residential properties in the American Southwest.

highlights.title: Strong Appreciation
highlights.body: Guard-gated communities consistently outperform the broader market in price appreciation due to limited supply, controlled inventory, and high demand from relocating buyers.

highlights.title: Privacy & Exclusivity
highlights.body: Guard-gated living provides a level of privacy and exclusivity that open communities cannot match. Many communities also have strict architectural review for cohesive aesthetics.

neighborhoods.name: The Ridges (Summerlin)
neighborhoods.desc: 793-acre enclave with Bear's Best golf, Red Rock Canyon views, and the highest per-square-foot prices in the valley.

neighborhoods.name: MacDonald Highlands
neighborhoods.desc: Henderson's premier mountainside community with DragonRidge Country Club and panoramic Strip views.

neighborhoods.name: Ascaya
neighborhoods.desc: 313 custom lots on the McCullough Range ridgeline with 360-degree valley views. Blue Heron desert-contemporary estates.

neighborhoods.name: Southern Highlands
neighborhoods.desc: Jack Nicklaus golf course, guard-gated estates, and panoramic mountain views in the south valley.

neighborhoods.name: Spanish Trail
neighborhoods.desc: Established 640-acre golf community with three 9-hole courses and mature estate lots in southwest Las Vegas.

neighborhoods.name: Anthem Country Club
neighborhoods.desc: Hale Irwin-designed championship course with luxury estates in the Henderson foothills.

faqs.q: How many guard-gated communities are in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The Las Vegas Valley has over 50 guard-gated residential communities spread across Summerlin, Henderson, Southern Highlands, North Las Vegas, and central Las Vegas. They range from affordable gated communities around $400K to ultra-luxury enclaves exceeding $20 million.

faqs.q: What is the most exclusive guard-gated community in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The Summit Club in Summerlin is the most exclusive, with invitation-only membership and homes from $5 million to $30 million+. The Ridges, Ascaya, and MacDonald Highlands (Dragon Rock) are also among the most prestigious guard-gated addresses in the valley.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees like in guard-gated communities?
faqs.a: HOA fees in guard-gated communities typically range from $200 to $1,500+ per month, depending on the community and amenities. Fees cover guard gate staffing, security patrols, common area maintenance, and community amenities like pools, fitness centers, and clubhouses.

faqs.q: Are guard-gated homes a good investment in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Yes. Guard-gated communities consistently outperform the broader Las Vegas market in price appreciation due to limited supply, controlled inventory, high-quality construction, and strong demand from relocating high-net-worth buyers.

faqs.q: Can I visit a guard-gated community before buying?
faqs.a: Yes. As your agent, I can arrange access through the guard gate for community tours and home showings. Guard-gated communities require pre-authorization for entry, which I coordinate directly with the community's security team.

faqs.q: Do all guard-gated communities have golf courses?
faqs.a: No. While many guard-gated communities feature championship golf (Spanish Trail, Red Rock Country Club, Canyon Gate, Southern Highlands), others like The Ridges, Ascaya, and Queensridge are purely residential enclaves focused on luxury living without on-site golf.

name: 55+ Active Adult Communities
overview: Las Vegas is one of the top retirement destinations in the United States, and the valley's 55+ active adult communities are a major reason why. With no state income tax, 300+ days of sunshine, world-class healthcare at facilities like the Cleveland Clinic Lou Ruvo Center, and an unmatched entertainment scene, Las Vegas provides retirees with a lifestyle that few cities can rival — all at a fraction of the cost of coastal markets.

The valley's premier 55+ communities are led by Sun City Summerlin and Sun City Anthem — two Del Webb developments that have set the national standard for active adult living. Both feature multiple recreation centers, championship golf courses, resort-style pools, fitness centers, and social calendars with 100+ chartered clubs and organizations. Homes range from $300,000 to $700,000+, with single-story floor plans designed for comfort and accessibility.

Newer 55+ options include Trilogy at Summerlin, Heritage at Stonebridge, Regency at Summerlin, and Ardiente in North Las Vegas. These communities offer modern architecture, updated amenities, and guard-gated security. Siena in Summerlin and Solera at Anthem provide boutique 55+ experiences within larger master-planned communities. Del Webb at Lake Las Vegas offers waterfront retirement living.

My team at Nevada Real Estate Group specializes in helping retirees find the right 55+ community in Las Vegas. We know which floor plans have the best views, which communities have the most active social scenes, and which HOAs deliver the best value. Whether you're downsizing from a family home or relocating from out of state, we'll guide you to the community that fits your lifestyle.

highlights.title: No State Income Tax
highlights.body: Nevada's no-income-tax policy means retirement income — Social Security, pensions, IRA distributions, capital gains — is not taxed at the state level. A major advantage for retirees from California, Illinois, and New York.

highlights.title: World-Class Recreation Centers
highlights.body: Sun City Summerlin and Sun City Anthem each feature 100,000+ square feet of amenity space with pools, fitness, arts studios, ballrooms, and meeting rooms for hundreds of clubs.

highlights.title: Championship Golf
highlights.body: 55+ communities offer preferred-rate access to championship golf courses including Highland Falls, Palm Valley, Eagle Crest, Anthem Course, and Revere Golf Club.

highlights.title: Single-Story Living
highlights.body: Nearly every home in Las Vegas 55+ communities is single-story, designed for comfort and accessibility with no-step entries, wider hallways, and low-maintenance desert landscaping.

highlights.title: Year-Round Sunshine
highlights.body: 300+ days of sunshine per year enable outdoor living, golf, pool time, and social activities year-round. Mild winters eliminate the need for seasonal migration.

highlights.title: Proximity to Healthcare
highlights.body: Multiple major hospital systems (Dignity Health, HCA, Sunrise), the Cleveland Clinic Lou Ruvo Center for Brain Health, and hundreds of specialists serve the Las Vegas Valley.

neighborhoods.name: Sun City Summerlin
neighborhoods.desc: The original Las Vegas 55+ community with 7,779 homes, three golf courses, four recreation centers, and one of the most active social calendars in the country.

neighborhoods.name: Sun City Anthem
neighborhoods.desc: 7,219 homes in southeast Henderson with two golf courses, three recreation centers, and 100+ clubs. Henderson's top-rated 55+ community.

neighborhoods.name: Trilogy at Summerlin
neighborhoods.desc: Newer guard-gated 55+ community in Summerlin with modern architecture, resort amenities, and a boutique social experience.

neighborhoods.name: Siena (Summerlin)
neighborhoods.desc: Guard-gated 55+ village within Summerlin South with a private clubhouse, pool, fitness center, and walking trails.

neighborhoods.name: Ardiente
neighborhoods.desc: Newer 55+ community in North Las Vegas offering strong value with modern floor plans, a community center, and easy I-15 access.

neighborhoods.name: Del Webb at Lake Las Vegas
neighborhoods.desc: Waterfront 55+ living at Lake Las Vegas with lake and mountain views, a recreation center, and access to Lake Las Vegas village shops and dining.

faqs.q: What are the best 55+ communities in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Sun City Summerlin and Sun City Anthem are the largest and most established. Trilogy at Summerlin and Heritage at Stonebridge offer newer construction with modern amenities. Siena, Regency at Summerlin, and Ardiente provide additional options across the valley.

faqs.q: What is the age requirement for 55+ communities?
faqs.a: At least one resident in the household must be 55 years of age or older. Most communities do not allow permanent residents under 19. Specific age and residency rules vary by community HOA.

faqs.q: How much do homes cost in Las Vegas 55+ communities?
faqs.a: Prices range from approximately $200,000 for smaller condos/attached homes to $800,000+ for premium single-family homes on golf course or mountain view lots. The most popular price range is $350,000–$550,000.

faqs.q: What amenities do Las Vegas 55+ communities offer?
faqs.a: Premier 55+ communities offer recreation centers with pools, fitness centers, tennis/pickleball courts, arts studios, ballrooms, and meeting rooms. Many also have championship golf courses, walking trails, community gardens, and 100+ social clubs and organizations.

faqs.q: Are Las Vegas 55+ communities guard-gated?
faqs.a: Some are, some aren't. Trilogy at Summerlin, Siena, Regency at Summerlin, and Heritage at Stonebridge are guard-gated. Sun City Summerlin and Sun City Anthem have controlled access but are not fully guard-gated with 24-hour staffed gates.

faqs.q: Is Las Vegas a good place to retire?
faqs.a: Yes. No state income tax on retirement income, 300+ days of sunshine, world-class healthcare, affordable housing compared to coastal cities, and an unmatched entertainment and dining scene make Las Vegas one of the top retirement destinations in the country.

name: Luxury Communities
overview: Las Vegas has emerged as one of the premier luxury real estate markets in the United States. Fueled by Nevada's no-income-tax policy, world-class amenities, year-round sunshine, and a growing influx of high-net-worth relocators from California and other high-tax states, the valley's luxury segment ($2 million+) has experienced record-breaking sales volume and price appreciation over the past five years.

The valley's ultra-luxury tier is anchored by The Summit Club in Summerlin — a Tom Fazio golf community with homes from $5 million to $30 million+ that operates on an invitation-only basis. The Ridges, also in Summerlin, offers semi-custom and custom estates from $2 million to $20 million+ with Red Rock Canyon views and Bear's Best golf. Ascaya in Henderson provides 313 custom lots on the McCullough Range with panoramic Strip views and Blue Heron construction.

MacDonald Highlands in Henderson is home to DragonRidge Country Club and features luxury communities like Dragon Rock, Neo, Vu Residences, and the Four Seasons Private Residences — a new high-rise development with branded residences starting at $3.67 million. Southern Highlands' Olympia Ridge and The Estates sections offer guard-gated luxury with Jack Nicklaus golf course access.

I've been active in the Las Vegas luxury market for 35 years, and I've never seen demand this strong. Remote work, tax migration, and the city's evolution into a world-class sports and entertainment destination have created a perfect storm for luxury real estate. Whether you're seeking a custom-build opportunity, a turnkey estate, or a branded high-rise residence, my team has the expertise and the network to deliver results at the highest level.

highlights.title: Tax-Free Luxury
highlights.body: No state income tax, no inheritance tax, and no estate tax make Nevada one of the most wealth-friendly states in America. High-net-worth buyers save six to seven figures annually vs. California.

highlights.title: Custom Estate Living
highlights.body: Ascaya, The Ridges, Dragon Rock, and Southern Highlands offer custom lot opportunities where buyers work with world-class architects and builders to create one-of-a-kind residences.

highlights.title: Branded Residences
highlights.body: Four Seasons Private Residences at MacDonald Highlands, Waldorf Astoria on the Strip, and other branded developments bring hotel-caliber service to residential ownership.

highlights.title: Championship Golf
highlights.body: Tom Fazio (Summit Club, DragonRidge), Jack Nicklaus (Southern Highlands, Bear's Best), and Arnold Palmer (Red Rock CC) courses provide world-class private golf.

highlights.title: Panoramic Views
highlights.body: Las Vegas luxury communities offer dramatic desert, mountain, Strip skyline, and golf course views that define the living experience. Many estates feature 270-degree panoramas.

highlights.title: Privacy & Security
highlights.body: Most luxury communities are guard-gated with 24-hour staffed security, controlled access, and strict architectural review boards that maintain property values and aesthetic standards.

neighborhoods.name: The Summit Club
neighborhoods.desc: The most exclusive address in Las Vegas. Tom Fazio golf, Discovery Land Company development, invitation-only membership.

neighborhoods.name: The Ridges
neighborhoods.desc: Summerlin's crown jewel with Bear's Best golf, Red Rock Canyon views, and the valley's highest per-square-foot prices.

neighborhoods.name: Ascaya
neighborhoods.desc: 313 custom lots on the McCullough Range ridgeline with panoramic Strip views and Blue Heron construction.

neighborhoods.name: MacDonald Highlands
neighborhoods.desc: Henderson's premier mountainside community with DragonRidge Country Club, Dragon Rock, Neo, and Four Seasons Private Residences.

neighborhoods.name: Southern Highlands
neighborhoods.desc: Olympia Ridge, The Estates, and Tuscan Cliffs offer guard-gated luxury with Jack Nicklaus golf in the south valley.

neighborhoods.name: Spanish Hills
neighborhoods.desc: Guard-gated custom estate community on the southwest valley rim with oversized lots and panoramic valley views.

faqs.q: What is considered luxury real estate in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: In the Las Vegas market, luxury is generally defined as $2 million and above. Ultra-luxury starts at approximately $5 million. The top tier — $10 million to $30 million+ — is concentrated in The Summit Club, The Ridges, Ascaya, and MacDonald Highlands (Dragon Rock).

faqs.q: What are the most expensive neighborhoods in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The Summit Club ($5M–$30M+), The Ridges ($2M–$20M+), Ascaya ($3M–$20M+), Dragon Rock ($5M–$15M+), and Olympia Ridge Estates ($2M–$10M+) command the highest prices in the Las Vegas Valley.

faqs.q: Why are wealthy people moving to Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Nevada's no-income-tax policy saves high-earners hundreds of thousands of dollars annually vs. California. Combined with world-class dining, entertainment, sports (Raiders, Golden Knights, Formula 1), year-round sunshine, and proximity to ski resorts, Las Vegas has become a premier relocation destination.

faqs.q: Can I build a custom home in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Yes. Ascaya, The Ridges (select lots), and Southern Highlands offer custom lot opportunities where buyers select an architect and approved builder. Blue Heron, Sun West Custom Homes, and others specialize in luxury custom construction in the valley.

faqs.q: What luxury high-rise options are available?
faqs.a: Waldorf Astoria Las Vegas, Veer Towers, Turnberry Place, Turnberry Towers, Park Towers, One Queensridge Place, and the forthcoming Four Seasons Private Residences at MacDonald Highlands offer luxury high-rise living with concierge services and resort amenities.

faqs.q: How is the luxury market performing in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The Las Vegas luxury market has seen record sales volume and price appreciation since 2020, driven by tax migration, remote work, and the city's growing cultural and sports infrastructure. Demand continues to outpace supply in the most desirable guard-gated communities.

name: New Construction Homes
overview: Las Vegas is one of the most active new construction markets in the country. National homebuilders including Toll Brothers, Lennar, KB Home, Taylor Morrison, Pulte Homes, Shea Homes, and Century Communities are actively building across the valley, delivering thousands of new homes annually in master-planned communities, guard-gated enclaves, and standalone neighborhoods.

The northwest corridor is the epicenter of new construction activity. Skye Canyon, Summerlin West (Grand Park, Kestrel, La Madre Peaks, Redpoint), and Sunstone are delivering new homes across every price point. In Henderson, Lake Las Vegas has seen a massive buildout with communities like Salerno Summit, Velaris, and Portofino. Cadence continues to expand with Symmetry Summit, Serenade, and other new neighborhoods.

Luxury new construction is concentrated in Summerlin's newest villages: Ascension at The Peaks, Carlisle Peak and Glenrock in Grand Park, and Astra at La Madre Peaks offer guard-gated new builds from $1 million to $5 million+. In Henderson, Neo and Cresta Rosa at MacDonald Highlands and the Four Seasons Private Residences represent the top of the new-build luxury market.

My team works directly with every major builder in the Las Vegas Valley. We can walk you through model homes, negotiate builder incentives, review construction contracts, and ensure your interests are protected throughout the build process. Whether you're a first-time buyer looking for a new home in the $400Ks or a luxury buyer seeking a $3 million+ guard-gated estate, we know which builders and communities are delivering the best value.

highlights.title: Major National Builders
highlights.body: Toll Brothers, Lennar, KB Home, Taylor Morrison, Pulte, Shea Homes, Century Communities, and Richmond American are all actively building across the Las Vegas Valley.

highlights.title: Every Price Point
highlights.body: New construction ranges from the mid-$300Ks for townhomes and starter homes to $5M+ for custom luxury estates in guard-gated enclaves like The Peaks, La Madre Peaks, and Neo.

highlights.title: Builder Incentives
highlights.body: Builders frequently offer significant incentives — rate buydowns, closing cost credits, design center upgrades, and lot premiums — especially in newer communities seeking to establish sales velocity.

highlights.title: Energy Efficiency
highlights.body: New homes in Las Vegas are built to current energy codes with high-efficiency HVAC, dual-pane Low-E windows, spray foam insulation, and solar-ready or solar-included packages.

highlights.title: Modern Floor Plans
highlights.body: Open-concept layouts, 10-foot ceilings, oversized islands, flex rooms, multi-generational suites, and disappearing glass wall systems define today's new construction in Las Vegas.

highlights.title: Warranty Protection
highlights.body: New homes come with builder warranties — typically 1 year on workmanship, 2 years on mechanical systems, and 10 years on structural components. Some builders offer extended warranty packages.

neighborhoods.name: Skye Canyon
neighborhoods.desc: The valley's most active new construction master plan with homes from multiple builders, Skye Center amenities, and top-rated schools.

neighborhoods.name: Summerlin West
neighborhoods.desc: Grand Park, Kestrel, La Madre Peaks, and Redpoint deliver Summerlin's newest homes with guard-gated and non-gated options.

neighborhoods.name: Lake Las Vegas
neighborhoods.desc: Massive new construction buildout with Salerno Summit, Velaris, Portofino, Lago Del Sol Hills, and more along the lake and hillsides.

neighborhoods.name: Cadence
neighborhoods.desc: Continuing expansion with Symmetry Summit, Serenade, Libretto, and other new neighborhoods from multiple builders.

neighborhoods.name: Inspirada
neighborhoods.desc: New construction phases continuing with parks, trails, and family-friendly design in south Henderson.

neighborhoods.name: Sunstone
neighborhoods.desc: Newer master plan in the northwest with Capella, Estrella, and Trilogy Sunstone (55+) communities from multiple builders.

faqs.q: Where is new construction happening in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The most active areas are the northwest corridor (Skye Canyon, Sunstone), Summerlin West (Grand Park, Kestrel, La Madre Peaks), Henderson (Lake Las Vegas, Cadence, Inspirada), and North Las Vegas (Tule Springs, Ardiente). Luxury new builds are concentrated in Summerlin's The Peaks, MacDonald Highlands, and Southern Highlands.

faqs.q: Should I use a real estate agent when buying new construction?
faqs.a: Absolutely. A buyer's agent costs you nothing — the builder pays the commission. Your agent can negotiate builder incentives, review contracts, attend inspections, and advocate for your interests throughout the build process. Builders' on-site agents represent the builder, not you.

faqs.q: What are typical builder incentives in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Common incentives include mortgage rate buydowns (often 1–2%), closing cost credits ($10K–$25K+), design center upgrade packages, lot premium discounts, and free appliance or smart-home packages. Incentives vary by builder and community.

faqs.q: How long does it take to build a new home in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Production homes (tract builders) typically take 6–9 months from contract to close. Semi-custom homes take 9–14 months. Full custom homes can take 14–24+ months depending on size, complexity, and permitting.

faqs.q: What is the cheapest new construction in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The most affordable new homes are townhomes and attached residences in communities like North Las Vegas (Tule Springs, Craig Ranch) and the southwest valley, starting in the low-to-mid $300Ks. Single-family new construction typically starts around $400K–$450K.

faqs.q: Are new homes in Las Vegas energy efficient?
faqs.a: Yes. New homes are built to current Nevada energy codes with high-efficiency HVAC systems, dual-pane Low-E windows, spray foam or blown-in insulation, and many include solar panels or are solar-ready. Monthly utility costs are typically 30–50% lower than comparable older homes.

name: High-Rise Condos
overview: Las Vegas offers one of the most exciting high-rise condo markets in the country. Over 20 residential towers line the Strip corridor and downtown, providing a lifestyle that combines resort-level amenities, panoramic skyline views, and the energy of the Las Vegas Strip with the convenience of lock-and-leave ownership. From entry-level studios under $200,000 to $10 million+ penthouses, the high-rise market serves every budget and lifestyle.

The Strip corridor features the valley's most prestigious addresses. Waldorf Astoria Las Vegas offers branded luxury with hotel services and Strip-front views from a 47-story tower. Veer Towers at CityCenter provide contemporary design and MGM resort pool access. Turnberry Place and Turnberry Towers deliver four-tower luxury with oversized floor plans and private balconies. Panorama Towers and The Martin offer value-oriented luxury with Strip proximity.

Downtown Las Vegas has emerged as a vibrant alternative with towers like Juhl, The Ogden, Newport Lofts, and Soho Lofts catering to buyers seeking urban walkability, the Arts District scene, and Fremont Street's revitalization. The forthcoming Cello Tower downtown will add ultra-luxury product to the downtown skyline.

High-rise living in Las Vegas is ideal for buyers who want low-maintenance ownership, travel frequently, or split time between multiple homes. Many towers offer concierge services, valet parking, resort pools, fitness centers, and on-site dining. As your agent, I can help you navigate HOA finances, building reserves, rental restrictions, and resale trends — critical factors that vary significantly from tower to tower.

highlights.title: 20+ Residential Towers
highlights.body: The Las Vegas Strip corridor and downtown offer over 20 residential high-rise towers, from branded luxury like Waldorf Astoria to value-oriented options like Signature at MGM Grand.

highlights.title: Panoramic Strip Views
highlights.body: Upper-floor units deliver dramatic panoramic views of the Las Vegas Strip, the Spring Mountains, Red Rock Canyon, and the surrounding desert landscape — day and night.

highlights.title: Resort Amenities
highlights.body: High-rise towers feature concierge services, valet parking, resort-style pools, fitness centers, spas, business centers, and in some cases, access to adjacent hotel amenities.

highlights.title: Lock-and-Leave Living
highlights.body: High-rise condos are ideal for buyers who travel frequently, split time between homes, or want zero exterior maintenance. Security, valet, and concierge handle the details.

highlights.title: Investment Potential
highlights.body: Select towers allow short-term or vacation rentals, creating income opportunities in one of the world's most visited cities. Rental policies vary — your agent should verify before purchase.

highlights.title: Every Price Point
highlights.body: From studios under $200K at Signature and Palms Place to $10M+ penthouses at Waldorf Astoria and Turnberry Place, the high-rise market offers something at every budget level.

neighborhoods.name: Waldorf Astoria Las Vegas
neighborhoods.desc: Branded residences with Waldorf Astoria hotel services, Strip-front location, and premium finishes. The valley's most prestigious condo address.

neighborhoods.name: Veer Towers
neighborhoods.desc: Twin 37-story towers at CityCenter with modern design, MGM resort pool access, and floor-to-ceiling glass.

neighborhoods.name: Turnberry Place
neighborhoods.desc: Four 36-story towers with oversized floor plans (2,000–7,000+ sq ft), private balconies, and resort amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Panorama Towers
neighborhoods.desc: Twin 33-story towers offering Strip views and luxury amenities at accessible price points. Popular with investors and part-time residents.

neighborhoods.name: One Queensridge Place
neighborhoods.desc: Two 18-story towers in Summerlin with 4,000–9,000+ sq ft residences, guard-gated entry, and mountain views away from the Strip.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Lofts
neighborhoods.desc: Juhl, The Ogden, Newport Lofts, and Soho Lofts offer urban living in the revitalized downtown core near the Arts District and Fremont Street.

faqs.q: What are the best high-rise condos in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: For luxury: Waldorf Astoria, Turnberry Place, Park Towers, and One Queensridge Place. For value: Panorama Towers, The Martin, and Allure. For Strip location: Veer Towers, Sky Las Vegas, and Vdara. For downtown: Juhl, The Ogden, and the forthcoming Cello Tower.

faqs.q: How much are HOA fees for Las Vegas high-rises?
faqs.a: HOA fees range from approximately $400/month for smaller units in value towers to $3,000+/month for large penthouse units in luxury towers. Fees typically cover water, sewer, trash, building insurance, common area maintenance, pools, fitness, security, and concierge.

faqs.q: Can I rent out my Las Vegas condo?
faqs.a: Rental policies vary by tower. Some towers (like Vdara and Signature) are condo-hotels and allow short-term rentals. Others (like Waldorf Astoria and Veer Towers) have minimum lease terms of 6–12 months. Always verify rental restrictions with your agent before purchasing.

faqs.q: Are Las Vegas condos a good investment?
faqs.a: It depends on the tower, the floor, and the rental policy. Well-located units with Strip views in buildings with strong HOA reserves and favorable rental policies have performed well. Towers with deferred maintenance, low reserves, or oversupply carry more risk. We analyze HOA financials as part of every condo transaction.

faqs.q: What is the cheapest high-rise condo in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Entry-level high-rise condos at Signature at MGM Grand, Palms Place, Jockey Club, and Marie Antoinette start under $200,000 for studios and smaller one-bedrooms. Downtown options like Juhl and Newport Lofts also offer entry points under $250,000.

faqs.q: Do Las Vegas condos have parking?
faqs.a: Yes. Most high-rise towers include assigned parking (typically 1–2 spaces per unit, sometimes more for penthouses). Many offer valet parking as well. Some older or smaller towers have limited parking inventory, so verify parking allocation before purchasing.

name: Golf Communities
overview: Las Vegas is one of the premier golf destinations in the world, and the valley's residential golf communities offer a lifestyle that combines championship course access with luxury living, year-round sunshine, and dramatic desert mountain scenery. Over 60 golf courses dot the valley, and dozens of residential communities are built directly around fairways, giving homeowners course-front living and preferred member access.

The luxury tier of golf communities includes some of the most exclusive addresses in Nevada. The Summit Club features a Tom Fazio-designed private course for an invitation-only membership. DragonRidge Country Club at MacDonald Highlands offers a Tom Fazio course with panoramic Strip views. Southern Highlands Golf Club is a private Jack Nicklaus Signature course. Red Rock Country Club in Summerlin has two Arnold Palmer-designed courses. Bear's Best at The Ridges features replicas of 18 of Jack Nicklaus' greatest holes.

Mid-range golf communities like Spanish Trail (three 9-hole courses), Canyon Gate Country Club (Ted Robinson design), Painted Desert (Jay Morrish design), and Stallion Mountain offer golf course living at accessible price points. For 55+ golfers, Sun City Summerlin (three courses), Sun City Anthem (two courses), and Sun City MacDonald Ranch provide active adult golf living with preferred resident rates.

Whether you're a serious golfer seeking private club membership, a casual player who wants to live on a fairway, or a buyer who simply appreciates the open space and views that golf courses provide, my team at Nevada Real Estate Group can match you with the right golf community. We know the membership structures, the initiation fees, the playing conditions, and the home values along every corridor.

highlights.title: 60+ Golf Courses
highlights.body: The Las Vegas Valley has over 60 golf courses ranging from world-class private clubs to affordable public courses. Year-round play with 300+ days of sunshine.

highlights.title: World-Class Designers
highlights.body: Courses designed by Tom Fazio, Jack Nicklaus, Arnold Palmer, Hale Irwin, Ted Robinson, and Jay Morrish are scattered across the valley's residential communities.

highlights.title: Private Club Membership
highlights.body: The Summit Club, DragonRidge, Southern Highlands Golf Club, Red Rock CC, and Anthem Country Club offer private membership with dining, social events, and priority tee times.

highlights.title: Course-Front Living
highlights.body: Homes along golf course fairways offer unobstructed green views, enhanced privacy from the setback, and premium resale value. Course-front lots command significant premiums.

highlights.title: Every Price Point
highlights.body: Golf community homes range from $250K at Los Prados and Stallion Mountain to $20M+ at The Summit Club and The Ridges. Every budget can access golf course living.

highlights.title: Year-Round Play
highlights.body: Las Vegas' mild winters and 300+ days of sunshine make it a 365-day golf destination. No seasonal closures, no rain-outs — just year-round desert golf.

neighborhoods.name: The Summit Club
neighborhoods.desc: Tom Fazio-designed private course, Discovery Land Company, invitation-only membership. The most exclusive golf community in Nevada.

neighborhoods.name: DragonRidge Country Club
neighborhoods.desc: Tom Fazio 18-hole course within MacDonald Highlands. Private membership with fine dining, resort pools, and tennis.

neighborhoods.name: Southern Highlands Golf Club
neighborhoods.desc: Jack Nicklaus Signature 18-hole course with guard-gated estates, mountain views, and a premier private clubhouse.

neighborhoods.name: Red Rock Country Club
neighborhoods.desc: Two Arnold Palmer-designed courses within Summerlin. Guard-gated with luxury homes and Red Rock Canyon views.

neighborhoods.name: Spanish Trail
neighborhoods.desc: Three 9-hole courses (Canyon, Sunrise, Lakes) within a 640-acre guard-gated community. Established luxury in southwest Las Vegas.

neighborhoods.name: Sun City Summerlin
neighborhoods.desc: Highland Falls, Palm Valley, and Eagle Crest courses within the valley's largest 55+ community. Preferred resident rates.

faqs.q: What are the best golf communities in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: For luxury/private: The Summit Club, DragonRidge (MacDonald Highlands), Southern Highlands Golf Club, and Red Rock Country Club. For mid-range: Spanish Trail, Canyon Gate, and Anthem Country Club. For 55+: Sun City Summerlin and Sun City Anthem. For value: Los Prados, Painted Desert, and Stallion Mountain.

faqs.q: Do I have to join the golf club to live in a golf community?
faqs.a: In most communities, membership is optional — you can live on the course without joining the club. However, some private clubs like The Summit Club require membership. Membership fees and structures vary significantly by community.

faqs.q: How much are golf memberships in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Initiation fees range from under $5,000 at semi-public courses to over $300,000 at ultra-exclusive clubs like The Summit Club. Monthly dues typically range from $300 to $1,500+. Many communities offer different membership tiers (social, golf, full, national).

faqs.q: Are golf course homes a good investment?
faqs.a: Yes, generally. Course-front homes command 10–25% premiums over comparable non-course homes due to the views, setback, and privacy. However, buyers should be aware that golf courses can potentially close — verify the course's financial health and ownership structure before purchasing.

faqs.q: Can I play golf year-round in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Yes. Las Vegas has no seasonal course closures. Summer tee times are typically very early morning (before 7am) due to heat, with afternoon rates deeply discounted. Fall through spring is peak season with ideal conditions.

faqs.q: What is the most affordable golf community in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Los Prados (from ~$300K), Painted Desert (from ~$400K), and Stallion Mountain (from ~$250K) offer the most affordable golf course living. Sun City Summerlin and Sun City MacDonald Ranch also provide golf-community living from ~$300K for 55+ buyers.

name: Vintage & Historic Neighborhoods
overview: Las Vegas has a rich architectural and cultural history that predates the Strip, and the city's vintage and historic neighborhoods offer a living experience unlike anything in the master-planned suburbs. From the guard-gated estates of Rancho Circle and Rancho Bel Air to the mid-century modern gems of Paradise Palms and Beverly Green, these neighborhoods provide character, oversized lots, mature landscaping, and proximity to the heart of the city.

Rancho Circle is the most exclusive vintage neighborhood in Las Vegas — a guard-gated enclave of 1-acre+ estate lots along Rancho Drive that has been home to Nevada's most prominent families since the 1940s and 1950s. Nearby Rancho Bel Air and Spanish Oaks offer similar estate-lot living with guard-gated security. The Scotch 80s neighborhood, named for its location within Section 80 of the original Las Vegas land grant, features mid-century homes on half-acre+ lots and has seen dramatic appreciation as buyers discover its proximity to downtown.

South of the Strip, Paradise Palms and Beverly Green showcase mid-century modern architecture from the 1960s — the era when Las Vegas was the playground of the Rat Pack. These neighborhoods have become highly sought-after by design-conscious buyers who appreciate exposed brick, butterfly rooflines, breeze blocks, and terrazzo floors. The Arts District (18b) in downtown Las Vegas blends vintage architecture with galleries, restaurants, and a creative energy that has made it the most dynamic urban neighborhood in the valley.

I've worked these neighborhoods for decades, and I've seen them transform from overlooked areas into some of the most desirable addresses in the city. Whether you're drawn to the character of a 1960s ranch on a half-acre lot or a fully renovated mid-century modern showpiece, my team can help you navigate the unique considerations of vintage home buying — foundation systems, electrical upgrades, plumbing, and the preservation vs. renovation decision.

highlights.title: Historic Character
highlights.body: Mid-century modern architecture, Rat Pack-era design, oversized lots, mature trees, and architectural styles you simply can't find in master-planned communities.

highlights.title: Oversized Lots
highlights.body: Vintage neighborhoods feature lots from 1/4 acre to 2+ acres — dramatically larger than modern subdivisions. Room for pools, guest houses, RV parking, and outdoor living.

highlights.title: Central Location
highlights.body: Most vintage neighborhoods are within 10 minutes of the Strip, downtown, and major employment centers. No 30-minute commutes from the suburban edges of the valley.

highlights.title: Guard-Gated Estates
highlights.body: Rancho Circle, Rancho Bel Air, Spanish Oaks, and Scotch 80s offer guard-gated or exclusive estate living within historic neighborhoods — old Vegas prestige with modern security.

highlights.title: Renovation Potential
highlights.body: Many vintage homes are prime candidates for renovation, offering buyers the opportunity to create a custom home on a premium lot at a fraction of new-build cost.

highlights.title: Arts & Culture
highlights.body: The Arts District (18b), Fremont East, and surrounding neighborhoods offer galleries, independent restaurants, breweries, and a creative community that defines modern downtown Las Vegas.

neighborhoods.name: Rancho Circle
neighborhoods.desc: Las Vegas' most exclusive vintage neighborhood with 1-acre+ estate lots, guard-gated security, and a history dating to the 1940s.

neighborhoods.name: Scotch 80s
neighborhoods.desc: Half-acre+ lots with mid-century and renovated homes in one of the most appreciated neighborhoods in the city. Minutes to downtown.

neighborhoods.name: Paradise Palms
neighborhoods.desc: 1960s-era homes with butterfly rooflines, breeze blocks, and terrazzo floors. A design-conscious buyer's dream near the Strip.

neighborhoods.name: Beverly Green
neighborhoods.desc: Charming mid-century neighborhood near the Strip with tree-lined streets, walkability, and growing renovation activity.

neighborhoods.name: Arts District (18b)
neighborhoods.desc: Downtown's most vibrant neighborhood with galleries, restaurants, lofts, and adaptive-reuse properties. The cultural heart of Las Vegas.

neighborhoods.name: Rancho Bel Air
neighborhoods.desc: Guard-gated estate community adjacent to Rancho Circle with large lots and luxury renovated homes.

faqs.q: What are the oldest neighborhoods in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The oldest residential neighborhoods include Rancho Circle (1940s), John S. Park (1930s), Huntridge (1940s), Scotch 80s (1950s–60s), and the downtown core. These neighborhoods predate the suburban master-planned era and offer historic character and oversized lots.

faqs.q: Are vintage homes in Las Vegas a good investment?
faqs.a: Many vintage neighborhoods have seen dramatic appreciation, especially Scotch 80s, Paradise Palms, and the Arts District. Central location, large lots, and renovation potential drive value. Buyers should budget for potential updates to plumbing, electrical, HVAC, and roofing.

faqs.q: What should I look for when buying a vintage home in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Key considerations include foundation condition (desert soil movement), electrical system capacity (many original homes have 100-amp service), plumbing materials (polybutylene pipes may need replacement), roof condition, and HVAC age. A thorough inspection by a specialist familiar with older homes is essential.

faqs.q: Where is the Arts District in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The Arts District (officially 18b) is located in downtown Las Vegas, centered around Main Street and Casino Center Boulevard between Charleston and Colorado. It features galleries, restaurants, breweries, First Friday events, and a growing residential community.

faqs.q: Can I renovate a mid-century modern home in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Yes. Mid-century modern renovation is a growing trend in Las Vegas. Neighborhoods like Paradise Palms, Beverly Green, and McNeil Estates are seeing significant renovation activity. Work with an architect who specializes in mid-century preservation to maintain the home's architectural character.

faqs.q: What is Rancho Circle?
faqs.a: Rancho Circle is Las Vegas' most prestigious vintage estate neighborhood — a guard-gated enclave of 1-acre+ lots along Rancho Drive near downtown. It has been home to Nevada's most prominent families since the 1940s. Homes range from $2 million to $10 million+.

name: Spanish Trail
overview: Spanish Trail is one of the most established and prestigious guard-gated golf communities in Las Vegas. Developed beginning in 1984, this 640-acre enclave in southwest Las Vegas features 27 holes of championship golf across three distinct 9-hole courses — Canyon, Sunrise, and Lakes — designed by Robert Trent Jones Jr. With approximately 1,500 homes ranging from luxury condos to custom estates, Spanish Trail offers a mature, tree-lined community with a lifestyle that blends country club living with convenient urban access.

The community is anchored by the Spanish Trail Country Club, a private club offering golf membership, fine dining, resort-style pools, tennis courts, a fitness center, and a robust social calendar. The three rotating 9-hole courses provide variety and challenge, with the Canyon course known for its dramatic elevation changes, the Sunrise course for its tight fairways and water features, and the Lakes course for its scenic lake views. Club membership is available but not required for homeownership.

Homes in Spanish Trail range from approximately $600,000 for updated condominiums and townhomes to over $3 million for custom estate homes on premium golf course lots. The community features a variety of architectural styles, from Mediterranean and Tuscan to transitional and contemporary, reflecting four decades of development. Many homes have been extensively renovated with modern finishes while maintaining the community's established, park-like setting with mature trees and lush landscaping.

Located minutes from the I-215 beltway and W Tropicana Avenue, Spanish Trail provides easy access to the Strip (15 minutes), Harry Reid Airport (15 minutes), and the shopping and dining destinations along W Flamingo Road and S Rainbow Boulevard. The community's central southwest location, established infrastructure, and country club lifestyle make it a perennial favorite among buyers seeking guard-gated golf living without the price tag of the valley's ultra-luxury communities.

highlights.title: 27 Holes of Championship Golf
highlights.body: Three 9-hole courses — Canyon, Sunrise, and Lakes — designed by Robert Trent Jones Jr. play in rotating 18-hole combinations for variety and challenge.

highlights.title: Guard-Gated Security
highlights.body: 24-hour staffed guard gate with controlled vehicular access, visitor management, and security patrols throughout the 640-acre community.

highlights.title: Private Country Club
highlights.body: Spanish Trail Country Club offers golf, fine dining, resort pools, tennis, fitness, and a full social calendar. Membership available but not required for homeownership.

highlights.title: Mature Landscaping
highlights.body: Four decades of growth have created a park-like setting with mature trees, lush landscaping, and tree-lined streets rare in the Las Vegas desert environment.

highlights.title: Central Location
highlights.body: 15 minutes to the Strip and airport, easy I-215 access, and proximity to shopping and dining along Tropicana, Flamingo, and Rainbow corridors.

highlights.title: Established Value
highlights.body: As one of the original luxury guard-gated communities in Las Vegas, Spanish Trail offers established infrastructure, proven HOA governance, and strong long-term appreciation.

faqs.q: What is the price range for homes in Spanish Trail?
faqs.a: Homes in Spanish Trail range from approximately $600,000 for updated condominiums and townhomes to over $3 million for custom estate homes on premium golf course lots. The most active price range is $800,000 to $1.5 million.

faqs.q: Is Spanish Trail guard-gated?
faqs.a: Yes. Spanish Trail is a fully guard-gated community with a 24-hour staffed guard gate, controlled vehicular access, visitor management, and security patrols throughout the 640-acre property.

faqs.q: What golf courses are in Spanish Trail?
faqs.a: Spanish Trail features 27 holes of championship golf across three 9-hole courses — Canyon, Sunrise, and Lakes — designed by Robert Trent Jones Jr. The courses play in rotating 18-hole combinations through the Spanish Trail Country Club.

faqs.q: Do I have to join the country club to live in Spanish Trail?
faqs.a: No. Golf and country club membership is available but not required for homeownership. Many residents enjoy the guard-gated security and community without club membership. Social and dining memberships are also available.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees in Spanish Trail?
faqs.a: HOA fees at Spanish Trail typically range from $350 to $800 per month depending on the property type (condo vs. single-family) and specific neighborhood. Fees cover guard gate staffing, security, common area maintenance, and community infrastructure.

faqs.q: What ZIP code is Spanish Trail in?
faqs.a: Spanish Trail spans ZIP codes 89113 and 89148 in southwest Las Vegas.

faqs.q: How does Spanish Trail compare to Southern Highlands?
faqs.a: Both are guard-gated golf communities in southwest Las Vegas. Spanish Trail is more established (1984 vs. 1999), has more mature landscaping, and offers a lower entry price. Southern Highlands is newer, has a Jack Nicklaus Signature course, and includes ultra-luxury estate sections with higher ceiling prices.

faqs.q: What schools serve Spanish Trail?
faqs.a: Spanish Trail is served by CCSD schools including Helen Jydstrup Elementary, Walter Johnson Middle School, and Durango High School. Top private options include Bishop Gorman (A+), The Meadows School (A+), and Faith Lutheran (A).

name: Canyon Gate Country Club
overview: Canyon Gate Country Club is a distinguished guard-gated golf community located in the heart of west Las Vegas. Developed beginning in 1989, this 350-acre community features an 18-hole Ted Robinson-designed championship golf course, approximately 900 homes, and a private country club that has served as one of Las Vegas' premier social and recreational destinations for over three decades.

The Ted Robinson-designed golf course at Canyon Gate is known for its strategic use of water features, mature tree-lined fairways, and challenging layout that rewards accurate shot-making. The course winds through the community, providing golf course frontage and views for many homes. The Canyon Gate Country Club offers full golf membership, social membership, and dining membership tiers, with amenities including the clubhouse restaurant, resort-style pool, tennis courts, and fitness center.

Homes in Canyon Gate range from approximately $700,000 for well-maintained resale homes to over $2 million for premium golf course-front estates. Architectural styles include Mediterranean, contemporary, and transitional designs, with many homes featuring updated interiors, pools, and outdoor living spaces. Lot sizes are generous by Las Vegas standards, and the community's mature landscaping creates a park-like atmosphere that distinguishes it from newer developments.

Canyon Gate's central west Las Vegas location is one of its strongest advantages. The community sits minutes from Summerlin's shopping and dining, the I-215 beltway, and the Strip corridor. W Sahara Avenue and S Hualapai Way provide multiple ingress and egress points. For buyers seeking guard-gated golf living in a central location with a proven track record, Canyon Gate Country Club delivers exceptional value and lifestyle.

highlights.title: Ted Robinson Golf Course
highlights.body: 18-hole championship course designed by Ted Robinson, featuring water features, mature tree-lined fairways, and a challenging layout that has hosted numerous regional tournaments.

highlights.title: Guard-Gated Security
highlights.body: 24-hour staffed guard gate with controlled access, visitor management, and security patrols throughout the 350-acre community.

highlights.title: Private Country Club
highlights.body: Canyon Gate Country Club offers golf, social, and dining memberships with a clubhouse restaurant, resort pool, tennis courts, and fitness center.

highlights.title: Central West Location
highlights.body: Minutes from Summerlin, the I-215 beltway, and the Strip. One of the most centrally located guard-gated golf communities in the valley.

highlights.title: Mature Community
highlights.body: 35+ years of established infrastructure, mature landscaping, proven HOA governance, and a stable, engaged resident community.

highlights.title: Value Proposition
highlights.body: Guard-gated golf living at a lower entry price than Summerlin and Henderson luxury communities. Strong resale values with room for appreciation.

faqs.q: What is the price range for homes at Canyon Gate Country Club?
faqs.a: Homes at Canyon Gate range from approximately $700,000 for attached patio homes and well-maintained resale properties to over $2 million for premium golf course-front estates with updated interiors.

faqs.q: Is Canyon Gate Country Club guard-gated?
faqs.a: Yes. Canyon Gate is a fully guard-gated community with a 24-hour staffed guard gate, controlled vehicular access, and security patrols throughout the 350-acre property.

faqs.q: Who designed the golf course at Canyon Gate?
faqs.a: The 18-hole championship course at Canyon Gate was designed by Ted Robinson, known for his creative use of water features and strategic course layouts. The course features mature tree-lined fairways and multiple water hazards.

faqs.q: Do I have to join the country club to live at Canyon Gate?
faqs.a: No. Country club membership is available but not required for homeownership. Canyon Gate Country Club offers golf membership, social membership, and dining membership tiers to accommodate different lifestyles and budgets.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees at Canyon Gate Country Club?
faqs.a: HOA fees at Canyon Gate typically range from $250 to $600 per month depending on property type. Fees cover guard gate staffing, security, common area maintenance, and community infrastructure.

faqs.q: What ZIP code is Canyon Gate Country Club in?

faqs.q: How does Canyon Gate compare to Spanish Trail?
faqs.a: Both are established guard-gated golf communities in west Las Vegas. Canyon Gate has an 18-hole course (vs. Spanish Trail's 27 holes), a slightly newer development era (1989 vs. 1984), and a comparable price range. Spanish Trail is larger (640 acres vs. 350 acres) with more homes.

faqs.q: What schools serve Canyon Gate Country Club?

name: Los Prados
overview: Los Prados is one of the most affordable guard-gated golf communities in the Las Vegas Valley. Developed beginning in 1983, this approximately 260-acre community in northwest Las Vegas features an 18-hole golf course, guard-gated security, and approximately 1,100 homes in a mature, well-established setting. With homes ranging from approximately $300,000 to $600,000, Los Prados provides country club living at a price point that makes it accessible to a wider range of buyers.

The Los Prados Golf Course is a par-70 layout that winds through the community, providing golf course frontage and views for many homes. The course features mature trees, water features, and a comfortable walking layout that appeals to golfers of all skill levels. The community clubhouse offers dining, social events, and a gathering space for residents. Golf membership is available and affordable compared to the valley's private clubs.

Homes in Los Prados include a mix of single-family homes, townhomes, and patio homes built across several development phases from the mid-1980s through the 1990s. Many homes have been updated and renovated with modern kitchens, bathrooms, and flooring. The community has an established, park-like feel with mature trees and landscaping that creates shade and visual appeal uncommon in newer Las Vegas communities.

Los Prados sits in the northwest valley near the intersection of Lone Mountain Road and N Decatur Boulevard, providing good access to the US-95 freeway, the Centennial Hills shopping corridor, and the amenities along N Rancho Drive. The community attracts a diverse buyer pool including active retirees, young professionals, and families seeking guard-gated security and golf course living at a value price point.

highlights.title: Affordable Golf Living
highlights.body: One of the most affordable guard-gated golf communities in Las Vegas. Golf course homes from approximately $300,000 — a fraction of what comparable communities charge.

highlights.title: Guard-Gated Security
highlights.body: 24-hour guard-gated access provides security and peace of mind at a fraction of the cost of the valley's luxury gated communities.

highlights.title: Mature Landscaping
highlights.body: 40+ years of growth have created a lush, tree-lined community with mature shade trees, green common areas, and a park-like atmosphere rare in Las Vegas.

highlights.title: Low HOA Fees
highlights.body: HOA fees of $150–$350/month are among the lowest of any guard-gated golf community in the valley. Excellent value for the amenities and security provided.

highlights.title: 18-Hole Golf Course
highlights.body: Par-70 championship layout with water features, mature fairways, and a comfortable walking design. Affordable golf memberships available to residents.

highlights.title: Northwest Location
highlights.body: Easy access to US-95, Centennial Hills shopping, and the growing northwest corridor. Close to Lone Mountain and outdoor recreation.

faqs.q: What is the price range for homes in Los Prados?
faqs.a: Homes in Los Prados range from approximately $300,000 for townhomes and patio homes to $600,000 for larger single-family homes on golf course lots. The most active price range is $350,000 to $500,000.

faqs.q: Is Los Prados guard-gated?
faqs.a: Yes. Los Prados is a guard-gated community with controlled access. It provides security at one of the most affordable price points of any gated golf community in the Las Vegas Valley.

faqs.q: What golf course is in Los Prados?
faqs.a: Los Prados features an 18-hole, par-70 golf course that winds through the community. The course has mature trees, water features, and a walkable layout. Affordable golf memberships are available to residents and the public.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees at Los Prados?
faqs.a: HOA fees at Los Prados typically range from $150 to $350 per month, making it one of the most affordable guard-gated golf communities in the valley. Fees cover guard gate staffing, common area maintenance, and community infrastructure.

faqs.q: Is Los Prados a 55+ community?
faqs.a: No. Los Prados is an all-ages community, though it does attract a significant number of active retirees due to the affordable golf course lifestyle. There are no age restrictions for residency.

faqs.q: What ZIP code is Los Prados in?
faqs.a: Los Prados is located in ZIP code 89130 in northwest Las Vegas.

faqs.q: How does Los Prados compare to Painted Desert?
faqs.a: Both are affordable guard-gated golf communities in northwest Las Vegas. Los Prados is slightly older (1983 vs. 1988) and has a lower entry price. Painted Desert has a Jay Morrish-designed course and slightly higher price points. Both offer excellent value for guard-gated golf living.

faqs.q: What schools serve Los Prados?
faqs.a: Los Prados is served by CCSD schools including Zel & Mary Lowman Elementary, Lied Middle School, and Legacy High School. Private options include Bishop Gorman (A+) and Faith Lutheran (A).

name: Eagle Crest
overview: Eagle Crest is one of three golf courses within Sun City Summerlin, the Las Vegas Valley's original and largest 55+ active adult community. The Eagle Crest Golf Course, designed by Billy Casper and Greg Nash, is an 18-hole executive layout that emphasizes playability and enjoyment over extreme difficulty, making it a favorite among Sun City residents who golf regularly. Homes surrounding the Eagle Crest course benefit from fairway views, open space setbacks, and the mature desert landscaping that defines this section of the community.

The Eagle Crest course features well-maintained fairways, strategic bunkering, and several water features that add both beauty and challenge. As an executive-length course (par 60), it plays shorter than the community's other two courses — Highland Falls and Palm Valley — making it popular for walkers, senior golfers, and players who prefer a quicker round. Preferred tee times and resident rates make golf an affordable daily activity.

Homes along the Eagle Crest corridor range from approximately $400,000 to $700,000, with the premium driven by golf course frontage, views, and lot position. Most are single-story Del Webb floor plans ranging from 1,400 to 2,800+ square feet, featuring open layouts, covered patios, and low-maintenance desert landscaping. Many homes have been updated with modern kitchens, flooring, and bathroom finishes.

As part of Sun City Summerlin, Eagle Crest residents have full access to four recreation centers, resort-style pools, fitness facilities, tennis and pickleball courts, and over 100 chartered clubs and organizations. The community's location in the western Summerlin corridor provides proximity to Downtown Summerlin's shopping and dining, Red Rock Canyon, and the I-215 beltway.

highlights.body: Billy Casper/Greg Nash-designed 18-hole executive course (par 60). Walkable, well-maintained, and perfect for daily play with preferred resident rates.

highlights.body: Full access to four recreation centers, resort pools, fitness, tennis, pickleball, ballrooms, and 100+ clubs. Over 100,000 square feet of amenity space.

highlights.body: In addition to Eagle Crest, Sun City Summerlin offers Highland Falls (18-hole championship) and Palm Valley (18-hole championship) for a total of 54 holes.

highlights.title: Single-Story Living
highlights.body: Del Webb floor plans from 1,400 to 2,800+ square feet, all single-story with covered patios, open layouts, and low-maintenance desert landscaping.

highlights.title: Summerlin Location
highlights.body: Minutes to Downtown Summerlin, Red Rock Canyon, and the I-215 beltway. All the benefits of the Summerlin corridor with the value of an established community.

highlights.title: Value Pricing
highlights.body: Golf course homes from $400K within Sun City Summerlin — a fraction of what comparable Summerlin golf-course homes cost in non-55+ communities.

faqs.q: What is Eagle Crest?
faqs.a: Eagle Crest is one of three golf courses within Sun City Summerlin, the valley's largest 55+ community. It is an 18-hole executive-length (par 60) course designed by Billy Casper and Greg Nash. Homes surrounding the course benefit from fairway views and open space.

faqs.q: What is the price range for homes near Eagle Crest?
faqs.a: Homes along the Eagle Crest golf course corridor range from approximately $400,000 to $700,000. Golf course-front homes command premiums for their fairway views and setback from neighbors.

faqs.q: Is Eagle Crest part of Sun City Summerlin?
faqs.a: Yes. Eagle Crest is one of three golf courses within the Sun City Summerlin 55+ community. Residents have full access to all Sun City Summerlin amenities including four recreation centers, pools, fitness, and 100+ clubs.

faqs.q: What is the age requirement for Eagle Crest?
faqs.a: As part of Sun City Summerlin, Eagle Crest follows the 55+ age requirement — at least one resident must be 55 or older, and no permanent residents under 19 are permitted.

faqs.q: How does Eagle Crest compare to Highland Falls and Palm Valley?
faqs.a: Eagle Crest is the executive-length course (par 60, shorter) while Highland Falls and Palm Valley are full championship-length courses (par 72). Eagle Crest is popular with walkers and daily golfers who prefer a quicker round. All three courses are available to Sun City Summerlin residents.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees at Eagle Crest?
faqs.a: As part of Sun City Summerlin, HOA fees range from $180 to $350 per month. Fees cover access to all four recreation centers, pools, fitness facilities, common area maintenance, and community programming.

faqs.q: What ZIP code is Eagle Crest in?
faqs.a: Eagle Crest is in ZIP code 89134 in Summerlin, Las Vegas.

faqs.q: Can non-residents play Eagle Crest?
faqs.a: The Sun City Summerlin golf courses are open to the public, but residents receive preferred tee times and discounted rates. Eagle Crest is a particularly popular choice for senior golfers due to its walkable executive layout.

name: Painted Desert
overview: Painted Desert is a well-established guard-gated golf community in northwest Las Vegas featuring an 18-hole Jay Morrish-designed championship course that is widely regarded as one of the best public-access courses in Nevada. Developed beginning in 1988, the approximately 320-acre community contains around 1,200 homes in a mature, well-maintained setting with exceptional mountain views and a central northwest Las Vegas location.

The Painted Desert Golf Club is the community's centerpiece — a par-72, 6,840-yard championship layout that uses the natural desert terrain to dramatic effect. Jay Morrish, known for his work at Troon North and other acclaimed desert courses, designed Painted Desert to blend seamlessly with the Mojave landscape while providing a challenging and visually stunning playing experience. The course is open to the public, with residents receiving preferred rates and tee times.

Homes in Painted Desert range from approximately $400,000 to $800,000, with the premium driven by golf course frontage, lot size, and renovation status. Architectural styles are predominantly Mediterranean and desert-contemporary, with many homes built in the late 1980s through the 1990s. A significant number of homes have been extensively updated with modern kitchens, flooring, and outdoor living spaces. Lot sizes are generous, and the community's mature landscaping provides shade and visual appeal.

Painted Desert's northwest location near W Ann Road and N Decatur Boulevard provides good access to the US-95 freeway, the Centennial Hills commercial corridor, and the growing northwest valley. The community is minutes from Centennial Hills Hospital, shopping at Centennial Center, and outdoor recreation at Floyd Lamb Park. For buyers seeking guard-gated golf living at a moderate price point with an award-winning course, Painted Desert is one of the valley's best values.

highlights.body: 18-hole, par-72 course designed by Jay Morrish. Ranked among the best public-access courses in Nevada with dramatic desert terrain and mountain views.

highlights.title: Mature Community
highlights.body: 35+ years of established landscaping, infrastructure, and community governance create a park-like setting with tree-lined streets and manicured common areas.

highlights.title: Moderate Price Point
highlights.body: Guard-gated golf homes from $400K — significantly less than comparable communities in Summerlin, Henderson, and Southern Highlands.

highlights.title: Generous Lot Sizes
highlights.body: Lot sizes in Painted Desert are generous by Las Vegas standards, with room for pools, outdoor kitchens, and expanded landscaping.

name: Desert Shores Villas
overview: Desert Shores Villas is a collection of lakefront and lake-adjacent condominium and townhome communities within the larger Desert Shores development in northwest Las Vegas. Built beginning in 1988 as part of the Greenspun Corporation's Desert Shores master plan, the Villas offer an affordable entry point to one of Las Vegas' most unique residential environments — a community centered around four man-made recreational lakes totaling over 30 acres of waterfront.

The Desert Shores Villas include several distinct condo and townhome complexes positioned along or near the community's lakes. Units range from approximately $200,000 for one-bedroom condos to $450,000 for larger waterfront townhomes with lake views, private patios, and updated interiors. Many units have been renovated with modern kitchens, new flooring, and updated bathrooms, while others present opportunities for buyers who want to customize.

Desert Shores' four lakes — Lake Jacqueline, Lake Ashley, Lake Tiffany, and Lake Serena — provide a lifestyle uncommon in the Las Vegas desert. Residents enjoy kayaking, paddleboarding, fishing (catch and release), and lakeside walking trails. The community's beaches, parks, and open spaces create a resort-like atmosphere, and the Desert Shores Community Association maintains the lakes, beaches, and common areas to a high standard.

The Villas' northwest Las Vegas location provides good access to the US-95 freeway, the shopping and dining along N Rancho Drive and W Lake Mead Boulevard, and the Summerlin corridor. The community is also close to The Lakes, another established community, and the growing Centennial Hills area. For buyers seeking waterfront living at a fraction of Lake Las Vegas pricing, Desert Shores Villas delivers remarkable value.

highlights.title: Lakefront Living
highlights.body: Four man-made recreational lakes — Jacqueline, Ashley, Tiffany, and Serena — provide waterfront living, lake views, and water activities unique in the Las Vegas desert.

highlights.title: Affordable Waterfront
highlights.body: Lake-view condos and townhomes from $200K — a fraction of what waterfront living costs at Lake Las Vegas or other lakefront communities nationwide.

highlights.title: Water Recreation
highlights.body: Kayaking, paddleboarding, fishing (catch and release), and lakeside walking on four interconnected lakes. Sandy beaches and lakeside picnic areas for residents.

highlights.title: Community Beaches
highlights.body: Sandy beaches maintained by the Desert Shores Community Association provide a resort-like atmosphere with lounge areas, shade structures, and lake access.

highlights.title: Low-Maintenance Living
highlights.body: Condo and townhome ownership with HOA-maintained exteriors, landscaping, and common areas. Ideal for busy professionals, snowbirds, and investors.

highlights.title: Central NW Location
highlights.body: Easy US-95 access, proximity to Summerlin and Centennial Hills shopping, and minutes from Red Rock Canyon and outdoor recreation.

faqs.q: What is the price range for Desert Shores Villas?
faqs.a: Desert Shores Villas range from approximately $200,000 for one-bedroom condos to $450,000 for larger waterfront townhomes with lake views and updated interiors. The most active price range is $250,000 to $375,000.

faqs.q: Are Desert Shores Villas on the lake?
faqs.a: Some units have direct lakefront positions with water views, while others are lake-adjacent or on interior streets. Lakefront units command higher prices. All residents have access to the four community lakes and beaches.

faqs.q: What activities are available on the Desert Shores lakes?
faqs.a: Residents can kayak, paddleboard, and fish (catch and release) on the four man-made lakes. Sandy beaches provide sunbathing and lakeside relaxation. Motorized boats are not permitted. Walking trails circle the lakes.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees at Desert Shores Villas?
faqs.a: HOA fees for the Villas typically range from $250 to $450 per month, covering exterior maintenance, landscaping, common area upkeep, lake and beach maintenance, and community amenities. Fees vary by specific complex.

faqs.q: Are Desert Shores Villas a good investment?
faqs.a: Desert Shores Villas offer strong rental demand due to their lakefront lifestyle and affordable pricing. The community attracts tenants seeking waterfront living at a fraction of Lake Las Vegas pricing. Verify rental restrictions in the specific HOA before purchasing for investment.

faqs.q: What is the difference between Desert Shores and Desert Shores Villas?
faqs.a: Desert Shores is the larger master-planned community with single-family homes, custom homes, and lakefront estates. Desert Shores Villas specifically refers to the condominium and townhome complexes within the community, offering a lower price point and maintenance-free lifestyle.

faqs.q: What ZIP code are Desert Shores Villas in?
faqs.a: Desert Shores Villas are located in ZIP code 89128 in northwest Las Vegas.

faqs.q: Is Desert Shores guard-gated?
faqs.a: Desert Shores is not guard-gated, though some individual condo complexes within the Villas have their own controlled access gates. The community does have a community association that maintains the lakes, beaches, and common areas.

name: Summerlin West
overview: Summerlin West is the newest and fastest-growing section of the Summerlin master-planned community, representing the western frontier of residential development in the Las Vegas Valley. Spanning over 5,600 acres along the base of the Spring Mountains, Summerlin West offers the most current architecture, the newest community amenities, and some of the most dramatic mountain and canyon views available in the Las Vegas metro area.

Developed by the Howard Hughes Corporation beginning around 2014, Summerlin West is organized into distinct villages including Grand Park, Kestrel, Kestrel Commons, Redpoint, Redpoint Square, Reverence, and La Madre Peaks. Each village contains multiple neighborhoods built by a mix of national and regional builders — Toll Brothers, Lennar, Shea Homes, Pulte, Taylor Morrison, and Woodside Homes among them. The variety of builders translates into a wide range of architectural styles, floor plans, and price points.

The village of Grand Park anchors Summerlin West with some of its most premium offerings, including the guard-gated luxury neighborhoods of Carlisle Peak and Glenrock. At the top of the price spectrum, La Madre Peaks delivers custom estate lots with panoramic Red Rock Canyon views, while Kestrel and Reverence provide attainable newer-construction options for move-up buyers and young families.

Summerlin West residents enjoy immediate proximity to Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area, the extensive Summerlin trail network, and the newest parks and recreation facilities in the Summerlin system. Downtown Summerlin — with its 125+ shops, restaurants, and the Las Vegas Ballpark — is a short drive east. The area is served by top-rated CCSD schools and several acclaimed private and charter school options.

highlights.title: Newest Construction
highlights.body: Summerlin West features the newest homes in the entire Summerlin master plan. Most neighborhoods were built from 2016 onward with modern open floor plans, energy-efficient systems, and smart-home technology.

highlights.title: Red Rock Canyon Proximity
