name: North Valley
overview: North Valley is a rapidly growing residential area in the northern section of North Las Vegas, spanning the corridor between Aliante to the west and the I-15 corridor to the east. The area has experienced explosive growth since the mid-2000s, transforming from open desert into a thriving suburban community with newer homes, improving schools, and expanding commercial infrastructure.

The majority of homes in North Valley were built between 2005 and the present, with several active new-construction communities still expanding. National builders including Lennar, KB Home, DR Horton, and Century Communities have been active in the area, delivering modern single-family homes with open floor plans, energy-efficient features, and community amenities. The newer vintage of the housing stock means lower maintenance costs and more contemporary design compared to older parts of the valley.

Pricing in North Valley ranges from approximately $300,000 for production homes to $500,000 for larger residences on premium lots. This represents one of the most affordable new-construction markets in the Las Vegas metro, attracting families, first-time buyers, and investors seeking modern homes at below-market prices. The value proposition is straightforward: comparable construction quality to Summerlin or Henderson at 20-30% lower prices.

North Valley's infrastructure continues to expand. The VA Hospital complex, growing retail along Craig Road and Centennial Parkway, and the planned northern extension of city services are bringing amenities closer to residents. The I-15 corridor provides direct commuting access to the Strip, downtown, and Henderson, while the 215 Beltway connects to the northwest valley. As North Las Vegas continues to mature, North Valley stands to benefit from appreciation driven by improving amenities and limited new land supply.

highlights.title: Affordable New Construction
highlights.body: Modern homes starting around $300K — one of the most affordable new-construction markets in the Las Vegas Valley. Comparable quality to Summerlin at significantly lower prices.

highlights.title: Modern Floor Plans
highlights.body: Predominantly built after 2005 with open floor plans, energy-efficient construction, smart home features, and contemporary finishes.

highlights.title: VA Hospital Complex
highlights.body: The VA Southern Nevada Healthcare System campus provides world-class medical services to veterans and adds institutional stability to the area.

highlights.title: Growing Commercial Corridor
highlights.body: Retail, dining, and services expanding rapidly along Craig Road, Centennial Parkway, and the I-15 corridor. Commercial growth is keeping pace with residential demand.

highlights.title: I-15 Commuter Access
highlights.body: Direct I-15 access for commuting to the Strip (20 min), downtown (15 min), and Henderson. The Beltway connects to the northwest valley.

highlights.title: Appreciation Potential
highlights.body: One of the fastest-appreciating areas in the Las Vegas Valley. Improving infrastructure and limited new land supply support continued upward price movement.

name: Cheyenne Corridor
overview: The Cheyenne Corridor is a well-positioned residential area in the northwest Las Vegas Valley, stretching along Cheyenne Avenue from the US-95 interchange westward toward the foothills. The corridor spans established neighborhoods from the 1990s through newer construction from the 2010s, creating a diverse residential landscape with a strong suburban character and improving commercial infrastructure.

The area's primary appeal is its balance of location and value. Situated between the established communities of Centennial Hills to the north and the Summerlin transition zone to the south, the Cheyenne Corridor benefits from the commercial infrastructure of both areas. Major shopping centers, grocery stores, medical offices, and dining establishments line Cheyenne Avenue and intersecting arterials like Durango Drive, Fort Apache Road, and Buffalo Drive.

Homes along the Cheyenne Corridor range from approximately $350,000 for 1990s-era single-family homes to $600,000 for newer or larger residences in premium sections. The housing stock includes a healthy mix of single-story and two-story single-family homes, townhomes, and some condo developments. Newer construction on the western end near the foothills commands the highest prices, while established sections closer to US-95 offer more affordable entry points.

The corridor's freeway access is a significant advantage. The US-95 interchange at Cheyenne provides fast commuting to downtown Las Vegas, the Strip, and Henderson, while the 215 Beltway connects to Summerlin and the western valley. For buyers seeking a well-connected northwest valley location with moderate pricing and established neighborhood infrastructure, the Cheyenne Corridor delivers consistent value.

highlights.body: Well-positioned between Centennial Hills and Summerlin with access to both communities' commercial infrastructure and amenities.

highlights.body: Direct freeway access at US-95 and proximity to the 215 Beltway. Fast commutes to downtown, the Strip, Summerlin, and Henderson.

highlights.body: Major retail, grocery, medical, and dining along Cheyenne Avenue and intersecting arterials. Everything is close to home.

highlights.body: From 1990s established homes to 2010s new construction. Single-family, townhome, and condo options at every price point in the $350K–$600K range.

highlights.body: Multiple well-maintained parks including the popular Charlie Frias Park with sports fields, trails, and playground facilities.

highlights.body: Northwest valley quality at moderate pricing. The Cheyenne Corridor offers similar construction to Summerlin and Centennial Hills at lower cost.

name: Silverstone
overview: Silverstone is a well-established residential community in central Henderson, located along the Eastern Avenue and Stephanie Street corridors between Green Valley Ranch and Anthem. The community was developed primarily in the early 2000s and features quality single-family homes, community parks, and a convenient location that provides access to some of Henderson's best shopping, dining, and medical facilities.

The community's name derives from the Silverstone Ranch and Silverstone Golf Club that once anchored the area. Today the residential neighborhoods feature a mix of single-family homes ranging from approximately $400,000 for smaller floor plans to $700,000 for larger homes on premium lots. The construction quality is typical of early 2000s Henderson development — solid block and stucco with tile roofing, open floor plans, and well-designed community spaces.

Silverstone's central Henderson location is arguably its greatest asset. The community is equidistant from Green Valley Ranch Resort to the north and the Anthem Marketplace to the south, with the Galleria at Sunset mall, Henderson Hospital, and numerous professional offices within a five-to-ten minute drive. The I-215 beltway and I-515/US-95 are both accessible, providing connectivity to every part of the Las Vegas Valley.

For families, Silverstone offers a combination of good schools, safe neighborhoods, and community amenities at price points that are more moderate than the premium guard-gated communities nearby. The area's mature landscaping, established HOAs, and proven infrastructure give it a settled, well-maintained character that newer communities in the eastern valley cannot yet match.

highlights.title: Central Henderson Location

name: Elkhorn
overview: The Elkhorn area is a well-established residential corridor in the northwest Las Vegas Valley, centered along Elkhorn Road between the 215 Beltway and the northern desert foothills. The area developed primarily in the 1990s and 2000s, resulting in mature neighborhoods with proven infrastructure, established landscaping, and a family-oriented suburban character.

Elkhorn Road itself serves as the community's spine, providing a direct north-south route through the area and connecting to major east-west arterials including Cheyenne Avenue, Alexander Road, and the 215 Beltway. The corridor benefits from growing commercial development with grocery stores, restaurants, medical offices, and retail services increasingly lining the route.

Homes in the Elkhorn area range from approximately $350,000 for established 1990s construction to $600,000 for larger or newer homes in the western sections near the foothills. The housing stock includes single-family homes in various sizes, with both single-story and two-story options available. Many homes feature the classic Nevada suburban design of the era — stucco exteriors, tile roofing, attached two-car garages, and desert-adapted landscaping.

The Elkhorn area is popular with families due to its proximity to multiple CCSD schools, community parks, and the overall safe suburban character of the neighborhoods. The area's elevation provides views of Lone Mountain, the Spring Mountains, and the broader valley, adding scenic value that flat-terrain communities closer to the valley floor cannot offer.

highlights.body: 1990s-2000s construction with mature landscaping, proven infrastructure, and settled neighborhood character. Well-maintained suburban communities throughout.

highlights.body: Elevated terrain provides views of Lone Mountain, the Spring Mountains, and the Sheep Range from many homes throughout the area.

highlights.body: Northwest valley location at moderate pricing. Similar quality to adjacent Summerlin villages and Centennial Hills at lower per-square-foot costs.

name: Fort Apache Corridor
overview: The Fort Apache Corridor is one of the most desirable north-south residential corridors in the Las Vegas Valley, stretching along Fort Apache Road from the southern I-215 interchange near Enterprise northward through Summerlin's western neighborhoods and into the Lone Mountain area. The corridor serves as a de facto dividing line between established central valley communities to the east and the premium Summerlin neighborhoods to the west.

Fort Apache Road has evolved from a secondary residential street into a major arterial with significant commercial development. Shopping centers, medical complexes, fitness facilities, and restaurants line the route, particularly at its intersections with major east-west arterials like Flamingo Road, Charleston Boulevard, Sahara Avenue, and Cheyenne Avenue. This commercial development has enhanced property values along the corridor while providing residents with walkable or short-drive access to daily necessities.

Housing along the Fort Apache Corridor spans a wide range. Southern sections near Enterprise and I-215 feature newer construction from the 2000s and 2010s, with single-family homes starting around $400,000. The mid-section near Spring Valley and Peccole Ranch offers established homes from the 1990s in the $500K-$650K range. Northern sections near Summerlin and the foothills command premium prices up to $800,000 or more, particularly for homes with mountain views.

The corridor's versatility is its greatest strength. Buyers at every price point and life stage can find suitable housing along Fort Apache, from first-time buyer townhomes to executive-level single-family homes. The consistent north-south connectivity, growing commercial infrastructure, and proximity to both Summerlin amenities and central valley services make the Fort Apache Corridor one of the most strategic residential addresses in Las Vegas.

highlights.title: Premier North-South Corridor
highlights.body: Fort Apache Road provides seamless north-south connectivity from Enterprise to Lone Mountain, linking major east-west arterials and freeway interchanges.

highlights.body: The western edge of the corridor borders Summerlin, giving residents access to Downtown Summerlin, Red Rock Casino, and Summerlin's extensive trail and park system.

highlights.body: Major shopping centers, medical complexes, restaurants, and fitness facilities at every major intersection. One of the best-served commercial corridors in the valley.

highlights.body: Homes from $400K to $800K+ accommodate every buyer profile. First-time buyers, families, executives, and investors all find suitable options along the corridor.

highlights.title: Quality Schools
highlights.body: Multiple well-rated CCSD schools and premier private schools including The Meadows School and Bishop Gorman are accessible from the corridor.

highlights.title: Consistent Appreciation
highlights.body: The Fort Apache Corridor has seen steady appreciation driven by its Summerlin proximity, commercial development, and limited west-side land supply.

name: Sun City Anthem South
overview: Sun City Anthem South is the southern expansion phase of the celebrated Sun City Anthem 55+ active adult community in Henderson. Developed by Del Webb beginning in 2001, this section extends the Sun City Anthem experience into the foothills of the McCullough Range, providing residents with slightly more elevated terrain, wider lot options, and dramatic desert views that complement the broader community's renowned amenity package.

Residents of Sun City Anthem South enjoy full access to all three Sun City Anthem recreation centers — the Anthem Center, Liberty Center, and Independence Center — totaling over 100,000 square feet of amenity space. This includes resort-style pools, fitness facilities, tennis and pickleball courts, ballrooms, arts studios, and meeting rooms for the community's 100+ chartered clubs and organizations. The social calendar is one of the most active of any 55+ community in the United States.

Homes in Sun City Anthem South range from approximately $350,000 for well-maintained single-story homes to $600,000 for larger floor plans on premium lots with golf course or mountain views. Virtually every home is single-story, designed for comfort and accessibility with open floor plans, covered patios, and attached two-car garages. Floor plans range from approximately 1,200 to 3,200 square feet.

The southern section's location provides convenient access to the Sun City Anthem golf courses, including the Anthem Course and the Revere Golf Club at Anthem. The community trail system extends into this section, connecting to Henderson's broader trail network for walking and cycling. The proximity to I-215 and the growing commercial corridor along Eastern Avenue ensures that shopping, dining, and medical services are within a short drive.

name: Summerlin South Area
overview: The Summerlin South Area encompasses the southern and western reaches of the Summerlin master-planned community, extending from the 215 Beltway westward to the Red Rock Canyon foothills. This section of Summerlin contains some of the most prestigious addresses in the Las Vegas Valley, including The Ridges, Red Rock Country Club, The Paseos, and numerous guard-gated enclaves that command the highest per-square-foot prices in Nevada.

Development in Summerlin South began in the late 1990s and has continued through the present day, with the Howard Hughes Corporation releasing new village phases on a carefully planned schedule. The area's western edge backs directly against the Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area, providing dramatic canyon and mountain views that are unavailable anywhere else in the valley. This proximity to protected federal land ensures that the western views will never be obstructed by future development.

The Summerlin South Area encompasses the full spectrum of Summerlin living, from production homes starting around $500,000 in villages like The Vistas and Discovery to ultra-luxury custom estates exceeding $20 million in The Ridges. Guard-gated communities within this zone include The Ridges, Red Rock Country Club, Canyon Fairways, Tournament Hills, and portions of The Hills. The variety of home styles, price points, and lifestyle options makes Summerlin South the most diverse luxury residential corridor in Nevada.

Downtown Summerlin serves as the commercial and social hub for the Summerlin South Area, featuring 125+ shops and restaurants, a minor league baseball stadium (Las Vegas Ballpark), an NHL practice facility, and the Red Rock Resort and Casino. The 215 Beltway provides direct connectivity to the Strip, Henderson, and the airport, while the Summerlin Parkway connects to US-95 and downtown Las Vegas. For buyers seeking the premier Las Vegas lifestyle, Summerlin South is the definitive address.

highlights.body: Western edge backs against Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area. Dramatic canyon and mountain views that are permanently protected from future development.

highlights.title: World-Class Golf
highlights.body: Bear's Best (Jack Nicklaus), Red Rock Country Club (Arnold Palmer), TPC Las Vegas, and Angel Park are all within or adjacent to the Summerlin South Area.

highlights.title: Premier Appreciation
highlights.body: Summerlin South consistently leads the Las Vegas Valley in per-square-foot values and long-term appreciation. Scarcity of western-facing land ensures sustained demand.

name: Valley Verde
overview: Valley Verde is a well-established residential community in central Henderson, situated between the Green Valley and Anthem corridors along the Warm Springs Road and Eastern Avenue intersection. Developed primarily in the mid-to-late 1990s, Valley Verde features quality single-family homes, mature landscaping, and a convenient location that has made it a perennial favorite among families and professionals seeking Henderson's quality of life at moderate price points.

The community's homes reflect the quality construction standards of 1990s Henderson development — solid block and stucco construction with tile roofing, attached garages, and well-designed floor plans. Properties range from approximately $400,000 for smaller single-family homes to $700,000 for larger residences on premium lots with upgraded features. The mature trees, established HOA standards, and neighborhood pride give Valley Verde a settled, well-maintained character.

Valley Verde's central Henderson location provides excellent access to the city's commercial infrastructure. The Warm Springs/Eastern intersection is a major commercial node with grocery, dining, medical, and retail services. The Galleria at Sunset mall, Green Valley Ranch Resort, and the shops along Stephanie Street are all within a short drive. Henderson's municipal services, consistently rated among the best in Nevada, serve the community.

For families, Valley Verde offers a combination of good schools, safe neighborhoods, and community amenities at price points more moderate than premium Henderson addresses like Seven Hills or Anthem Country Club. The area's established infrastructure, walkable design, and proven property value performance make it a low-risk, high-quality choice for buyers seeking long-term value in Henderson.

highlights.body: Consistent property value performance through multiple market cycles. Established infrastructure and Henderson's quality of life support long-term appreciation.

name: Stonebridge Area
overview: The Stonebridge area of Summerlin West represents one of the newest and most active new-construction zones in the entire Summerlin master plan. Located along the western edge of the Las Vegas Valley near the intersection of Far Hills Avenue and Flamingo Road, this area has seen explosive growth since 2018 with multiple national and regional builders delivering homes across a range of price points from the mid-$600s to over $1 million.

What makes the Stonebridge area particularly attractive is its position within Summerlin West, the newest association of the Howard Hughes Corporation's flagship master-planned community. Buyers here get the benefit of brand-new infrastructure, modern floor plans with energy-efficient construction, and proximity to Summerlin's expanding western amenities including new parks, trails, and the expanding trail system that connects to Red Rock Canyon's foothills.

The area features production homes from builders including Toll Brothers, Lennar, Pulte, Taylor Morrison, and Woodside Homes. Floor plans range from approximately 1,800 to 4,500 square feet, with both single-story and two-story options. Many neighborhoods within the Stonebridge area offer courtyard entries, multi-generational suites, and upgraded desert contemporary architecture that reflects the modern Summerlin West aesthetic.

For buyers seeking new construction in Summerlin without the ultra-luxury price tag of communities like The Peaks or La Madre Peaks, the Stonebridge area delivers modern design, quality construction, and the full Summerlin lifestyle at a more accessible price point. The western location also means proximity to Red Rock Canyon, Downtown Summerlin, and the Summerlin trail system that active buyers value.

highlights.body: New construction in Summerlin's western expansion corridor has shown strong appreciation as the area builds out and amenities mature. The Summerlin brand supports long-term value.

faqs.a: Yes. The Stonebridge area is located within Summerlin West, the newest association of the Howard Hughes Corporation's Summerlin master plan. Residents benefit from all Summerlin amenities, trails, parks, and community programming.

faqs.a: Multiple national and regional builders are active in the Stonebridge area including Toll Brothers, Lennar, Pulte, Taylor Morrison, Woodside Homes, and Shea Homes. Several 55+ communities including Del Webb's Stonebridge and Trilogy at Summerlin are also in this area.

name: Anthem Center
overview: Anthem Center is the heart of the Anthem master-planned community in Henderson, encompassing the core neighborhoods that surround the main community amenities including Anthem Center Park, the community recreation center, and the commercial corridor along Eastern Avenue. This central section of Anthem offers a well-established residential environment with homes ranging from approximately $500,000 to $800,000, attracting families, professionals, and retirees who want Anthem's renowned quality of life without the premium of the guard-gated enclaves.

Originally developed by Del Webb as part of the broader Anthem master plan beginning in 1998, the Anthem Center neighborhoods feature solid construction typical of the late 1990s and early 2000s building boom. Homes range from approximately 1,500 to 3,500 square feet, with single-story and two-story options available. Desert landscape, tile roofs, and stucco exteriors define the architectural character, with many homes backing to community parks, trails, or natural desert open space.

The central location within Anthem provides walkable or short-drive access to Anthem Center Park — one of the best community parks in Henderson — which features a splash pad, playground, basketball courts, walking trails, and open turf. The Anthem Hills Park, Anthem Fitness Trail, and multiple pocket parks add additional recreation options. Shopping, dining, and services along Eastern Avenue and in the Anthem Village shopping center keep daily conveniences close.

Anthem Center's appeal lies in its combination of established community character, strong schools, excellent parks, and a price point that sits below the guard-gated Anthem Country Club and Anthem Highlands sections. For buyers who want to live in one of Henderson's most desirable master-planned communities without spending $800K+, the Anthem Center neighborhoods deliver outstanding value and an enviable lifestyle.

highlights.body: One of Henderson's premier community parks featuring a splash pad, playground, basketball courts, walking trails, and open turf. The recreational heart of the Anthem community.

highlights.body: Located in the core of the Anthem master plan with immediate access to community amenities, shopping, dining, and services along Eastern Avenue.

highlights.body: Zoned for some of Henderson's best-rated CCSD schools including Del E. Webb Middle School and Coronado High School, plus access to top-rated charter and private options.

highlights.title: Mountain & Desert Views
highlights.body: Many homes enjoy views of the McCullough Range, Black Mountain, and the surrounding desert landscape. Elevation provides sweeping panoramas from upper-floor rooms.

highlights.title: Value in a Premier Community
highlights.body: Entry to the Anthem lifestyle from approximately $500K — well below the guard-gated enclaves while still enjoying all Anthem parks, trails, and community amenities.

highlights.title: Proven Long-Term Value
highlights.body: Anthem has been one of Henderson's strongest-appreciating communities since its inception. The master plan's quality infrastructure and amenities support lasting value.

faqs.a: Homes in the Anthem Center area range from approximately $500,000 to $800,000. The core neighborhoods offer established single-family homes from 1,500 to 3,500 square feet, with pricing dependent on lot size, views, and upgrades.

faqs.q: Is Anthem Center guard-gated?
faqs.a: No. The Anthem Center neighborhoods are not guard-gated. However, they are part of the broader Anthem master-planned community and benefit from all Anthem HOA amenities, parks, and trails. Guard-gated options within Anthem include Anthem Country Club and Sun City Anthem.

faqs.a: Anthem Center is served by CCSD schools including Elise L. Wolff Elementary (9/10), Del E. Webb Middle School (7/10), and Coronado High School. Private options include Henderson International School and Bishop Gorman High School (A+).

faqs.q: What amenities are in Anthem Center?
faqs.a: Anthem Center Park is the community's recreational heart with a splash pad, playground, basketball courts, and walking trails. The Anthem Fitness Trail, Anthem Hills Park, and multiple pocket parks provide additional recreation. Shopping and dining are available along Eastern Avenue.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees in Anthem Center?
faqs.a: HOA fees in the Anthem Center neighborhoods typically range from $100 to $250 per month, covering common area maintenance, parks, trails, and community amenities. This is significantly lower than the guard-gated Anthem enclaves.

faqs.q: How does Anthem Center compare to Anthem Country Club?
faqs.a: Anthem Center offers the core Anthem lifestyle at a lower price point ($500K–$800K vs $1.2M–$8M+). Anthem Country Club is guard-gated with a private golf course, while Anthem Center is non-gated but shares the same parks, trails, schools, and community infrastructure.

faqs.q: When was Anthem Center built?
faqs.a: Most homes in the Anthem Center area were built between 1998 and 2006 during the original phases of the Anthem master plan. Construction quality is typical of Del Webb and Pulte Group production homes from this era.

name: Stephanie Street Corridor
overview: The Stephanie Street corridor is one of Henderson's most well-established and centrally located residential areas, running through the heart of the city along Stephanie Street from Lake Mead Parkway south to Horizon Ridge Parkway. This broad corridor encompasses dozens of individual neighborhoods built primarily between 1990 and 2005, offering a diverse mix of single-family homes, townhomes, and condominiums at price points that represent some of the best value in Henderson.

What makes the Stephanie Street corridor particularly attractive is its central Henderson location with convenient access to virtually everything the city offers. The Galleria at Sunset mall, Henderson's largest shopping center, anchors the northern end. Multiple grocery stores, restaurants, medical offices, and services line the corridor. The Henderson Convention Center, Henderson Pavilion performing arts venue, and multiple city parks are all within a few minutes' drive.

Housing stock along the Stephanie Street corridor ranges from approximately 1,200-square-foot townhomes and condos starting around $350,000 to larger single-family homes reaching $600,000 or more. The area attracts a diverse buyer pool including first-time buyers, young families, professionals working in Henderson's growing job base, and investors seeking solid rental properties in a high-demand area with strong schools and low vacancy rates.

For buyers priced out of Henderson's premium master-planned communities like Anthem, Seven Hills, or Green Valley Ranch, the Stephanie Street corridor offers a legitimate Henderson address with good schools, excellent access, and a price point that allows entry into one of the most desirable cities in the Las Vegas metro. The central location also means shorter commute times to the Strip, Harry Reid Airport, and Henderson's major employers.

highlights.body: In the heart of Henderson with walkable access to Galleria at Sunset, dining, medical, and services. Central to everything the city offers.

highlights.body: Entry to a Henderson address from approximately $350K. One of the most accessible price points in one of Nevada's most desirable cities.

highlights.body: Single-family homes, townhomes, and condominiums ranging from 1,200 to 3,000+ sq ft. Something for every buyer profile and budget.

highlights.body: Zoned for well-rated CCSD schools with access to Henderson's private and charter school network. Multiple options within a short drive.

highlights.title: Convenient Commutes
highlights.body: Central location provides 15-20 minute access to the Strip, Harry Reid Airport, and major Henderson employers via I-215 and Eastern Avenue.

highlights.title: Investment Potential
highlights.body: Strong rental demand, low vacancy rates, and Henderson's reputation as a top-rated city drive solid investment fundamentals. Proven appreciation in the central corridor.

name: Southwest Ranch
overview: Southwest Ranch is an upscale residential area in the southwestern portion of the Las Vegas Valley, characterized by larger lot sizes, custom and semi-custom homes, and a semi-rural atmosphere that sets it apart from the more densely developed master-planned communities nearby. Located south of Blue Diamond Road and west of Las Vegas Boulevard South, the area offers a sense of space and privacy that is increasingly rare in the Las Vegas metro.

Homes in Southwest Ranch range from approximately $600,000 for semi-custom production homes to over $1.5 million for larger custom estates on half-acre to one-acre lots. The architectural character tends toward Southwestern and Mediterranean styles, though newer construction has introduced desert contemporary design. Many properties feature equestrian facilities, RV storage, detached workshops, and the kind of generous lot sizes that attract buyers seeking a retreat atmosphere within city limits.

The area's appeal lies in its combination of relative seclusion and practical accessibility. Southern Highlands is just minutes to the east, the I-15 corridor provides 15-20 minute access to the Strip and Harry Reid Airport, and the Mountains Edge commercial corridor along Blue Diamond Road offers grocery, dining, and essential services. Yet the lower density, desert views, and mountain backdrop create a distinct character that feels removed from the urban core.

Southwest Ranch attracts executives, entrepreneurs, equestrian enthusiasts, and families who want more land and privacy than a typical master-planned community offers. The lack of guard gates keeps the price-to-square-footage ratio attractive, while the larger lots and custom construction provide an estate-like living experience. For buyers who prioritize space, views, and autonomy over HOA-managed amenities, Southwest Ranch delivers a compelling lifestyle.

highlights.title: Large Estate-Size Lots

name: Buffalo Ranch
overview: Buffalo Ranch is a well-established residential area in northwest Las Vegas, centered along Buffalo Drive between Cheyenne Avenue and Ann Road. Developed primarily between 1998 and 2008, the area offers a solid mix of single-family homes at price points ranging from approximately $400,000 to $700,000 — placing it squarely in the sweet spot for families and professionals seeking quality northwest Las Vegas living without the premium of a master-planned community.

The Buffalo Ranch area sits in a convenient northwest corridor with quick access to the US-95 freeway, providing 15-20 minute commutes to the Strip, Downtown Las Vegas, and the Summerlin business corridor. The area is flanked by major retail centers, grocery stores, and services along Buffalo Drive, making daily errands walkable or a short drive for most residents. Centennial Hills Hospital is nearby, and the TPC Las Vegas golf course is just to the northwest.

Housing stock in Buffalo Ranch consists primarily of two-story and single-story single-family homes ranging from approximately 1,600 to 3,200 square feet. Most homes feature stucco and tile roof construction typical of the era, with desert-adapted landscaping. Many neighborhoods have mature trees and established community character. The area attracts repeat buyers who want a proven neighborhood with good schools, convenient access, and steady appreciation at a moderate price point.

Buffalo Ranch's location in the northwest growth corridor means the area continues to benefit from surrounding development. New retail, restaurants, and medical facilities along the 215 Beltway and Centennial Parkway have significantly improved the area's convenience in recent years. For buyers who want a northwest Las Vegas address with established value and room to grow, Buffalo Ranch delivers a strong combination of location, livability, and long-term investment potential.

highlights.body: Central northwest location with quick access to US-95, the 215 Beltway, and major retail corridors along Buffalo Drive. 15-20 minute commutes to the Strip and Summerlin.

highlights.title: Established Family Neighborhoods
highlights.body: Mature residential area with proven community character. Tree-lined streets, established landscaping, and neighborhoods with strong neighborhood identity.

highlights.body: Access to well-rated CCSD schools plus northwest Las Vegas' growing private and charter school network. Multiple options within a short drive.

highlights.body: Quality single-family homes from $400K–$700K in the northwest corridor. Excellent value for the location and school zone compared to nearby master plans.

highlights.title: Growing Retail & Services
highlights.body: Expanding commercial development along Buffalo Drive, the 215 Beltway, and Centennial Parkway. Grocery, dining, medical, and entertainment all nearby.

highlights.title: Steady Appreciation
highlights.body: The northwest growth corridor has driven consistent appreciation in the Buffalo Ranch area. Surrounding development continues to enhance property values.

name: Aliante North
developer: Del Webb / Pulte Group / American Nevada Corp
overview: Aliante North encompasses the northern portion of the Aliante master-planned community in North Las Vegas, one of the most successful and well-regarded master plans in the city. Located north of Aliante Parkway and extending toward Elkhorn Road, this section features newer construction, wider lots, and a more open feel compared to the community's earlier southern phases.

Built primarily between 2003 and 2010 with some newer infill, Aliante North offers single-family homes ranging from approximately 1,400 to 3,200 square feet at price points between $350,000 and $550,000. The area benefits from its proximity to the Aliante Nature Discovery Park, a 20-acre park featuring walking trails, playgrounds, and wetland areas that provide a green oasis in the desert landscape.

The Aliante master plan was designed with community amenities at its core, and the northern section is no exception. The Aliante Casino + Hotel + Spa, Aliante Golf Club (an 18-hole course open to the public), Aliante Library, and multiple neighborhood parks are all within the community. The 215 Northern Beltway provides convenient freeway access for commuters working on the Strip, in Summerlin, or in Henderson.

Aliante North attracts first-time buyers, young families, and value-conscious purchasers who want the structure and amenities of a master-planned community at North Las Vegas pricing. The area offers significantly more square footage per dollar than comparable communities in Las Vegas or Henderson, and the quality of the community infrastructure — parks, trails, golf, casino — rivals many higher-priced neighborhoods across the valley.

name: El Dorado Springs
overview: El Dorado Springs is an established residential area in central North Las Vegas, encompassing neighborhoods along and near the Carey Avenue and Cheyenne Avenue corridors between Losee Road and the I-15 freeway. Developed primarily between 1995 and 2008, the area offers some of the most affordable single-family housing in the Las Vegas metro, with homes ranging from approximately $300,000 to $500,000.

The El Dorado Springs area features a mix of single-family homes, townhomes, and some multi-family development. Single-family homes typically range from 1,200 to 2,600 square feet, with both single-story and two-story floor plans available. Construction quality varies by builder and era, with homes from the early 2000s generally featuring the best construction standards. Desert landscaping, tile roofs, and stucco exteriors are standard throughout.

What makes El Dorado Springs compelling for buyers is the combination of affordability and improving infrastructure. North Las Vegas has invested heavily in community parks, public safety, and commercial development in recent years. The Eldorado neighborhood park system, Craig Ranch Regional Park (one of the best parks in the metro), and expanding retail corridors along Craig Road and Las Vegas Boulevard North have significantly improved the area's livability.

El Dorado Springs attracts first-time buyers, working families, military personnel from nearby Nellis Air Force Base, and investors seeking positive cash flow rental properties. The area's affordability premium over comparable Las Vegas and Henderson neighborhoods — often $100K–$200K less for similar square footage — makes it an important entry point for buyers who want homeownership in the Las Vegas Valley at today's pricing.

highlights.body: Single-family homes from $300K in the Las Vegas metro. $100K–$200K less than comparable square footage in Las Vegas or Henderson for similar construction quality.

highlights.title: Improving Infrastructure
highlights.body: North Las Vegas has invested heavily in parks, public safety, and commercial corridors. New retail and restaurants continue to open along Craig Road and Las Vegas Boulevard North.

highlights.title: Craig Ranch Regional Park
highlights.body: One of the best parks in the Las Vegas metro is just minutes away. 170 acres with sports facilities, skate park, water play area, dog park, and walking trails.

highlights.title: Nellis AFB Proximity
highlights.body: Convenient to Nellis Air Force Base for military families. Short commute to one of the largest employers in the north Las Vegas Valley.

highlights.title: Freeway Access
highlights.body: I-15 and the 215 Northern Beltway provide convenient access to the Strip (20 min), Downtown (15 min), and Henderson (25 min).

highlights.title: Investment Fundamentals
highlights.body: Strong rental demand, low vacancy rates, and North Las Vegas' growth trajectory create solid investment returns. Affordable entry with positive cash flow potential.

name: The Lake at Las Vegas
developer: Lake Las Vegas Resort / Raintree Investment Corp
overview: The Lake at Las Vegas is the waterfront centerpiece of the Lake Las Vegas resort community in Henderson, a unique master-planned destination built around a 320-acre man-made lake surrounded by desert mountains. The waterfront properties along the lake offer a lifestyle unlike anything else in the Las Vegas Valley — private docks, lake views, waterside dining, and a Mediterranean village atmosphere that feels more like an Italian lake resort than a desert community.

Waterfront homes and condominiums at The Lake at Las Vegas range from approximately $500,000 for lakeside condos and townhomes to well over $2 million for custom lakefront estates with private docks. The architectural character leans heavily Mediterranean and Tuscan, consistent with the community's overall design language. Many waterfront properties feature covered patios, outdoor kitchens, and private boat access to the 320-acre lake.

The Lake Las Vegas community offers resort-style amenities including two golf courses (the Jack Nicklaus-designed Reflection Bay and the former South Shore course), a Westin Lake Las Vegas hotel, the MonteLago Village with shops and restaurants, and direct lake access for kayaking, paddleboarding, and electric boating. The waterfront promenade, beach areas, and lakeside dining create a vacation atmosphere year-round.

The Lake at Las Vegas is approximately 20 miles east of the Strip, providing a genuine retreat experience while remaining connected to the metro. Henderson's services, shopping, and medical facilities along the Lake Mead Parkway corridor are 15-20 minutes west. For buyers seeking a unique waterfront lifestyle in the desert — whether as a primary residence, second home, or vacation property — The Lake at Las Vegas offers something that simply cannot be replicated elsewhere in Southern Nevada.

highlights.title: 320-Acre Lake Living
highlights.body: Built around a 320-acre man-made lake — the largest privately owned lake in Nevada. Kayaking, paddleboarding, electric boating, and waterfront dining year-round.

highlights.title: Waterfront & Dock Access
highlights.body: Select homes feature private docks with direct lake access. Lakefront patios, water views, and a lifestyle that rivals coastal living in the desert.

highlights.title: Reflection Bay Golf Club
highlights.body: Jack Nicklaus-designed championship course overlooking the lake. One of Nevada's most scenic and highly rated public courses.

highlights.title: MonteLago Village
highlights.body: Mediterranean-style village center with shops, restaurants, and lakeside dining. Live entertainment, seasonal events, and a genuine resort atmosphere.

highlights.title: Resort Amenities
highlights.body: Westin Lake Las Vegas, spa facilities, beach areas, waterfront promenade, and community pool. A resort-caliber lifestyle without the resort price tag.

highlights.title: Unique Desert Waterfront
highlights.body: The only waterfront living community of its kind in the Las Vegas Valley. Irreplaceable asset with limited inventory and growing demand.

faqs.q: What is the price range at The Lake at Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Waterfront and lake-community properties at Lake Las Vegas range from approximately $500,000 for condos and townhomes to over $2 million for custom lakefront estates with private docks. The average single-family home on the lake sells between $800K and $1.5M.

faqs.q: Is Lake Las Vegas guard-gated?
faqs.a: Portions of Lake Las Vegas are guard-gated, including the South Shore enclave and several newer developments. The MonteLago Village and some lakeside areas are accessible to the public for dining and recreation.

faqs.q: Can you boat on the lake?
faqs.a: Yes. Lake Las Vegas allows electric boating, kayaking, paddleboarding, and other non-motorized watercraft. Select properties have private docks with direct lake access. Motorized boats with combustion engines are not permitted.

faqs.q: What golf courses are at Lake Las Vegas?
faqs.a: Reflection Bay Golf Club is a Jack Nicklaus-designed championship course overlooking the lake, open to the public. It is consistently ranked among Nevada's best courses.

faqs.q: How far is Lake Las Vegas from the Strip?
faqs.a: Lake Las Vegas is approximately 30 minutes from the Las Vegas Strip via Lake Mead Parkway and I-215/I-15. Harry Reid Airport is about 25 minutes away.

faqs.q: What ZIP code is Lake Las Vegas in?
faqs.a: Lake Las Vegas is in ZIP code 89011 in Henderson, Nevada.

faqs.q: Is Lake Las Vegas a good investment?
faqs.a: Lake Las Vegas offers unique waterfront living that cannot be replicated elsewhere in the Las Vegas Valley. The limited inventory, resort amenities, and growing demand for lakefront properties support long-term value. Many properties also perform well as vacation rentals.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees at Lake Las Vegas?
faqs.a: HOA fees at Lake Las Vegas range from approximately $200 to $800 per month depending on the specific community. Fees cover lake maintenance, common areas, guard gate staffing (where applicable), and community amenities.

name: Rhodes Ranch South
overview: Rhodes Ranch South is the southern section of the Rhodes Ranch guard-gated community in southwest Las Vegas, one of the valley's most popular communities for families, professionals, and golfers seeking guard-gated living at an accessible price point. Located south of Russell Road near Durango Drive, this section features the community's newer phases of construction and proximity to the southern amenities of the broader Rhodes Ranch development.

Rhodes Ranch is anchored by a Ted Robinson-designed 18-hole championship golf course that winds through the community, adding significant green space, views, and value. Rhodes Ranch South residents enjoy access to the community's extensive amenities including the golf club, resort-style community pools, fitness center, tennis courts, basketball courts, and a network of walking trails that connect throughout the guard-gated community.

Homes in Rhodes Ranch South range from approximately $350,000 for smaller single-family homes and attached products to $600,000 for larger detached homes on premium lots. Floor plans range from approximately 1,400 to 3,000 square feet. The newer phases in the southern section tend to feature more contemporary floor plans, updated builder standards, and modern desert landscaping compared to the community's earlier northern phases.

For buyers seeking guard-gated community living with golf, pools, and resort amenities at a price point below the valley's premium gated communities, Rhodes Ranch South delivers exceptional value. The southern location provides quick access to I-215 and I-15 for commuters, while the Mountains Edge and Southern Highlands commercial corridors along Blue Diamond Road and Southern Highlands Parkway keep shopping and dining close.

highlights.body: 24-hour staffed guard gate with controlled access throughout the community. One of the most affordable guard-gated communities in the Las Vegas Valley.

highlights.body: 18-hole championship golf course designed by Ted Robinson winds through the community. Green fairways and water features add beauty and value to surrounding homes.

highlights.body: Multiple community pools, fitness center, tennis courts, basketball courts, and walking trails. A country club lifestyle at a fraction of country club pricing.

highlights.body: Entry to a guard-gated golf community from approximately $350K. Among the most accessible gated community price points in the Las Vegas metro.

highlights.title: Southwest Location
highlights.body: Quick access to I-215 and I-15 for commuters. Mountains Edge and Southern Highlands shopping, dining, and services within minutes.

highlights.title: Consistent Value
highlights.body: Rhodes Ranch has maintained strong demand and consistent values due to the guard-gated entry, golf course, and resort amenities. A proven community with a loyal resident base.

name: Warm Springs
overview: The Warm Springs area is a well-established residential corridor in southwest Las Vegas, centered along Warm Springs Road between Durango Drive and Las Vegas Boulevard South. Named for the historic warm springs that once flowed in the area, this broad neighborhood encompasses dozens of subdivisions built between the mid-1990s and mid-2000s, offering a diverse mix of single-family homes, townhomes, and condominiums at price points between $350,000 and $650,000.

The Warm Springs corridor occupies a strategic position in the southwest valley with excellent access to I-215 and I-15, placing the Strip approximately 15 minutes away and Harry Reid Airport within 20 minutes. The area is flanked by commercial corridors along Durango Drive and Rainbow Boulevard, providing abundant grocery, dining, medical, and retail options. The newer commercial development along the I-215 corridor has added significant convenience in recent years.

Housing stock in the Warm Springs area ranges from approximately 1,200-square-foot attached products to 3,200-square-foot single-family homes on standard lots. The architectural character is typical of the Las Vegas building boom era — stucco, tile roofs, desert landscaping — with some newer infill projects bringing contemporary design. Many neighborhoods feature community pools, pocket parks, and sidewalk connectivity.

Warm Springs appeals to a broad buyer base including families, professionals, commuters, and investors. The combination of central southwest location, freeway access, diverse housing options, and moderate pricing makes it one of the more practical residential areas in the valley. For buyers who want to be close to everything without paying master-planned community premiums, Warm Springs delivers a compelling balance of value and convenience.

highlights.title: Strategic Southwest Location
highlights.body: Central southwest position with I-215 and I-15 access. 15 minutes to the Strip, 20 minutes to the airport, and surrounded by commercial corridors.

highlights.title: Diverse Housing Options
highlights.body: Single-family homes, townhomes, and condominiums across a wide price range. Entry-level to move-up options in a single corridor.

highlights.title: Established Community Character
highlights.body: Mature neighborhoods with proven community identity. 20+ years of established landscaping, neighbor relationships, and community stability.

highlights.title: Abundant Commercial Services
highlights.body: Retail, grocery, dining, and medical along Durango Drive, Rainbow Boulevard, and the I-215 corridor. Daily conveniences within minutes.

highlights.title: Easy Commute Access
highlights.body: I-215 Beltway access provides quick routes to Summerlin, Henderson, and the Strip. One of the best-connected areas in the southwest valley.

highlights.title: Practical Value
highlights.body: Quality homes from $350K in the southwest corridor without master-planned community premiums. Strong rental demand and consistent appreciation.

faqs.a: Homes in the Warm Springs area range from approximately $350,000 for townhomes and smaller properties to $650,000 for larger single-family homes. The average single-family home sells between $400K and $500K.

faqs.q: Is Warm Springs a good location for commuters?

faqs.q: Is Warm Springs a master-planned community?
faqs.a: No. The Warm Springs area is a collection of individual neighborhoods and subdivisions along the Warm Springs Road corridor, not a single master-planned community. Each neighborhood may have its own HOA.

faqs.a: HOA fees vary by neighborhood and range from $50 to $175 per month. Some standalone homes have no HOA. Townhome and condo communities typically have higher fees covering shared amenities.

faqs.a: The Warm Springs area is surrounded by commercial corridors along Durango Drive, Rainbow Boulevard, and the I-215 interchange areas. Multiple grocery stores, restaurants, and retail centers are within a few minutes' drive.

faqs.a: Yes. Warm Springs offers strong investment fundamentals including affordable entry points, consistent rental demand, central location, and freeway access. The diverse housing stock allows investment at multiple price points.

name: Solera Henderson
overview: Solera Henderson is a guard-gated 55+ active adult community in Henderson, developed by Del Webb and offering an intimate, resort-style lifestyle for active adults seeking community, convenience, and low-maintenance living. Located near the intersection of Eastern Avenue and Horizon Ridge Parkway, Solera provides a quieter alternative to the larger Sun City communities while delivering the same Del Webb quality and amenity standards.

The community features approximately 1,200 single-story homes ranging from about 1,200 to 2,400 square feet, with prices between $350,000 and $550,000. Floor plans emphasize open layouts, covered patios, and low-maintenance desert landscaping. The single-story-only design throughout the community ensures accessibility and aging-in-place capability for all residents.

Solera Henderson's clubhouse is the social heart of the community, featuring a resort-style pool and spa, fitness center, ballroom, library, arts and crafts studios, and card rooms. The community offers an active social calendar with clubs, classes, events, and organized activities that foster connection among residents. Walking trails, a community garden, and outdoor recreation areas round out the amenities.

The Henderson location places Solera within easy reach of Henderson's excellent medical facilities, shopping centers, restaurants, and parks. The Galleria at Sunset, The District at Green Valley Ranch, and Henderson Hospital are all within a short drive. For active adults who want guard-gated security, social programming, and a Henderson address at a price point below the larger Sun City communities, Solera delivers an exceptional value proposition.

name: Aliante Corridor
overview: The Aliante Corridor encompasses the residential neighborhoods along and surrounding Aliante Parkway in North Las Vegas, extending from the Aliante master plan's perimeter outward through adjacent subdivisions and newer development. This growth corridor represents one of the most active areas of residential development in the north Las Vegas Valley, with new homes, commercial projects, and infrastructure improvements reshaping the area.

Homes in the Aliante Corridor range from approximately $300,000 to $500,000, offering some of the best value per square foot in the Las Vegas metro. Housing stock includes a mix of 1990s-2000s established subdivisions and newer infill construction from the 2010s-2020s. Floor plans range from 1,200 to 2,800 square feet in both single-story and two-story configurations, with desert landscaping and tile roofs standard throughout.

The corridor benefits from its proximity to the Aliante master plan's amenities — the Aliante Casino + Hotel + Spa, Aliante Golf Club, Aliante Nature Discovery Park, and the Aliante Library — without the higher HOA fees of the master plan itself. The 215 Northern Beltway and I-15 provide freeway access for commuters, while the Aliante Parkway commercial corridor offers grocery, dining, and essential services.

For buyers seeking affordable homeownership in a growth corridor with improving amenities and infrastructure, the Aliante Corridor delivers strong fundamentals. North Las Vegas is the fastest-growing city in Nevada, and the areas surrounding the Aliante master plan are benefiting directly from that growth. New schools, parks, and commercial development continue to enhance the area's livability and investment potential.

highlights.title: New Commercial Development
highlights.body: Expanding retail, restaurants, and services along Aliante Parkway and the 215 corridor. Daily conveniences improving annually.

highlights.title: Investment Fundamentals
highlights.body: North Las Vegas' population growth, job creation, and infrastructure investment drive strong rental demand and appreciation across the Aliante Corridor.

name: Chinatown
overview: Las Vegas Chinatown is a vibrant cultural and commercial district stretching approximately two miles along Spring Mountain Road between Valley View Boulevard and Rainbow Boulevard. What began in 1995 with the opening of the Chinatown Plaza has exploded into one of the most dynamic dining, shopping, and cultural corridors in the American West, now widely regarded as having the best Asian food scene of any city in the United States outside of the San Francisco and New York metro areas.

The residential neighborhoods surrounding Chinatown offer an eclectic mix of single-family homes, townhomes, and condominiums at price points ranging from approximately $250,000 to $600,000. The housing stock varies significantly, from 1970s ranch-style homes on larger lots to modern townhome developments built in the 2010s-2020s. This diversity of housing creates opportunities for first-time buyers, investors, professionals, and anyone who values walkable access to one of the best restaurant scenes in Las Vegas.

Chinatown's central location is one of its strongest assets. The area sits just west of the Strip, providing 5-10 minute access to the resort corridor, and is flanked by major freeways including I-15 and US-95. Commuters working on the Strip, in Downtown, or in the medical district along Rancho Drive benefit from extremely short commutes. The UNLV campus, the Strip, and Downtown are all within a 10-15 minute drive.

For buyers who value dining, culture, walkability, and central location over master-planned community amenities, Chinatown offers a lifestyle that is truly unique in Las Vegas. The district has undergone remarkable revitalization, with ongoing restaurant openings, commercial investment, and residential development driving both energy and property values. The area represents one of the most interesting emerging neighborhoods in the valley for buyers seeking character and convenience.

highlights.title: Premier Dining District
highlights.body: Widely considered the best Asian food corridor in the western United States. Hundreds of restaurants spanning Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Vietnamese, Thai, Filipino, and fusion cuisines.

highlights.title: Central Strip-Adjacent Location
highlights.body: Just west of the Las Vegas Strip with 5-10 minute access to the resort corridor. One of the most centrally located residential areas in the valley.

highlights.title: Cultural Vibrancy
highlights.body: A living cultural district with specialty markets, tea houses, bakeries, bookstores, and community events. Year-round Lunar New Year and cultural celebrations.

highlights.title: Diverse Housing Options
highlights.body: From 1970s ranch homes to modern townhomes, the area offers diverse styles and price points. Entry-level to move-up options in a single corridor.

highlights.title: Short Commutes
highlights.body: 5-10 minutes to the Strip, 10 minutes to Downtown, 10 minutes to UNLV. Among the shortest average commute times in the Las Vegas metro.

highlights.title: Emerging Value
highlights.body: Ongoing commercial investment and revitalization are driving property values. One of the most dynamic emerging neighborhoods in Las Vegas.

name: Arts District / 18b
developer: Various Developers / City of Las Vegas
overview: The Arts District — officially known as 18b, the Las Vegas Arts District — is an 18-block creative and cultural neighborhood in Downtown Las Vegas, bounded approximately by Las Vegas Boulevard to the east, Interstate 15 to the west, Charleston Boulevard to the north, and Sahara Avenue to the south. Designated by the City of Las Vegas in 1998, the district has undergone a remarkable transformation from an industrial area to one of the most vibrant and exciting neighborhoods in the valley.

The Arts District features a distinctive mix of galleries, studios, breweries, cocktail bars, vintage shops, street art, and creative businesses alongside a growing residential inventory that includes converted lofts, modern townhomes, mid-century renovations, and ground-up contemporary construction. Housing ranges from approximately $200,000 for studio condos and lofts to $800,000+ for custom modern homes and premium loft conversions.

First Friday — the monthly arts walk that draws thousands of visitors — has become one of Las Vegas' signature cultural events, and the surrounding businesses and restaurants have created a year-round neighborhood energy that extends well beyond the monthly event. The district's brewery scene (Able Baker, Astronomy Alehouse, Nevada Brew Works), cocktail bars (Velveteen Rabbit, Herbs & Rye), and restaurants have made it a dining and nightlife destination in its own right.

For buyers seeking an urban, walkable, creative lifestyle that is distinctly different from Las Vegas' master-planned suburbs, the Arts District offers something truly unique. The neighborhood attracts artists, entrepreneurs, young professionals, remote workers, and lifestyle-focused buyers who value character, walkability, and proximity to Downtown's growing employment and entertainment scene. Property values have appreciated significantly as the district matures, though entry points remain accessible compared to comparable urban neighborhoods in other major metros.

highlights.title: Creative & Cultural Hub
highlights.body: 18 blocks of galleries, studios, street art, and creative businesses. First Friday arts walk draws thousands monthly. A living cultural district unlike anything else in Las Vegas.

highlights.title: Walkable Urban Living
highlights.body: One of the only truly walkable neighborhoods in Las Vegas. Walk to galleries, breweries, restaurants, and nightlife from your front door.

highlights.body: Converted lofts, modern townhomes, mid-century renovations, and new construction. From $200K studio condos to $800K+ custom modern homes.

highlights.title: Brewery & Dining Scene
highlights.body: Craft breweries, cocktail bars, and chef-driven restaurants throughout the district. Able Baker, Astronomy Alehouse, Velveteen Rabbit, and more.

highlights.title: Downtown Proximity
highlights.body: Adjacent to Downtown Las Vegas, Fremont East, and the emerging DTLV tech and startup scene. Walking distance to downtown employment and entertainment.

highlights.title: Rapid Appreciation
highlights.body: The Arts District has experienced some of the strongest appreciation in the Las Vegas metro as the neighborhood matures. Early buyers have seen significant returns.

faqs.q: What is the price range in the Arts District?
faqs.a: Homes in the Arts District range from approximately $200,000 for studio condos and lofts to $800,000+ for custom modern homes and premium conversions. The average unit sells between $300K and $500K.

faqs.q: Where is the Arts District in Las Vegas?
faqs.a: The Arts District (18b) occupies 18 blocks in Downtown Las Vegas, bounded approximately by Las Vegas Boulevard, I-15, Charleston Boulevard, and Sahara Avenue. It is just south of Fremont Street.

faqs.q: What is First Friday?
faqs.a: First Friday is a monthly arts walk held on the first Friday of every month in the Arts District. Galleries open their doors, food trucks line the streets, live music plays, and thousands of visitors explore the district's creative culture.

faqs.q: Is the Arts District safe?
faqs.a: The Arts District has improved significantly in recent years with increased foot traffic, business investment, and residential development. As with any urban neighborhood, awareness is important. The active street life and growing resident base contribute to safety.

faqs.q: What types of housing are in the Arts District?
faqs.a: The Arts District offers converted industrial lofts, modern townhomes, mid-century home renovations, new construction condos, and mixed-use live/work spaces. It is one of the most architecturally diverse neighborhoods in Las Vegas.

faqs.q: Is the Arts District walkable?
faqs.a: Yes. The Arts District is one of the only truly walkable neighborhoods in Las Vegas. Residents can walk to galleries, restaurants, breweries, coffee shops, and nightlife destinations within the district.

faqs.q: Is the Arts District a good investment?
faqs.a: The Arts District has seen some of the strongest appreciation in the Las Vegas metro as the neighborhood continues to develop. The unique character, downtown location, and limited inventory create favorable conditions for long-term value growth.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees in the Arts District?
faqs.a: HOA fees vary widely from $100 to $400 per month depending on the building type. Condo and loft buildings typically have higher fees covering shared amenities. Standalone homes and townhomes may have lower or no HOA fees.

name: Horizon Ridge Corridor
overview: The Horizon Ridge corridor is one of Henderson's most desirable established residential areas, stretching along Horizon Ridge Parkway from Eastern Avenue east to Gibson Road and beyond. This broad corridor encompasses dozens of well-built neighborhoods developed primarily between 1998 and 2010, offering quality single-family homes with mountain views, excellent parks, and convenient access to Henderson's best shopping, dining, and medical facilities.

Homes along the Horizon Ridge corridor range from approximately $400,000 for smaller single-family homes to $800,000 for larger homes on premium lots with views. Floor plans typically range from 1,800 to 3,500 square feet, with both single-story and two-story options available. The area features quality construction from builders including Pulte, KB Home, DR Horton, and Beazer, with desert landscaping, tile roofs, and well-maintained HOA standards throughout.

The corridor's central Henderson position is a major draw. Horizon Ridge Parkway provides a direct east-west arterial connecting to I-215 and I-15 for easy commuter access, while the Anthem, Inspirada, and MacDonald Ranch master-planned communities are all within a few minutes' drive. Henderson Hospital, St. Rose Dominican Hospital, and multiple medical offices are along the corridor. Shopping and dining options at The District at Green Valley Ranch and the Horizon Ridge shopping centers keep daily needs close.

For buyers seeking an established Henderson address with good schools, mountain views, and convenient access at a price point below the guard-gated communities, the Horizon Ridge corridor offers compelling value. The area's proven track record of appreciation, strong community character, and central location make it one of the most practical and livable areas in the Henderson market.

highlights.title: Central Henderson Position
highlights.body: Strategic east-west corridor connecting to I-215, I-15, and major Henderson destinations. Central to everything Henderson offers.

highlights.title: Mountain & Valley Views
highlights.body: Many homes enjoy views of the McCullough Range, Black Mountain, and the Las Vegas Valley. Elevation provides sweeping panoramas from multiple neighborhoods.

highlights.title: Medical & Commercial Access
highlights.body: Henderson Hospital, St. Rose Dominican, and multiple medical offices along the corridor. The District at GVR and major retail within minutes.

highlights.title: Proven Appreciation
highlights.body: The Horizon Ridge corridor has appreciated consistently through market cycles. Henderson's reputation and infrastructure anchor long-term property values.

name: Anthem Hills
overview: Anthem Hills is the elevated premium section of the Anthem master-planned community in Henderson, encompassing the hillside neighborhoods south of Anthem Parkway and west of Anthem Country Club. Perched on the foothills of the McCullough Range, Anthem Hills offers some of the most dramatic views in the entire Anthem community — sweeping panoramas of the Las Vegas Valley, the Strip skyline, and the surrounding desert mountains from homes positioned at higher elevations than the valley floor.

Homes in Anthem Hills range from approximately $600,000 for established single-family homes to over $1 million for larger hillside properties with premium views and upgraded finishes. Floor plans typically range from 2,200 to 4,000 square feet, with both single-story and two-story options. The elevated positioning means many homes enjoy unobstructed views that lower-elevation properties in Anthem Center cannot match.

The architectural character in Anthem Hills reflects the early-2000s Henderson building standards, with quality stucco and stone construction, tile roofs, and desert landscaping. Many homes have been updated by owners with modern finishes, outdoor living spaces, and pool additions. The hillside positioning creates varied lot sizes and configurations, with some properties featuring walkout basements — a rarity in Las Vegas — and dramatic rear elevation views.

Anthem Hills provides a step up from the Anthem Center neighborhoods in terms of views, lot sizes, and home quality, while remaining more accessible than the guard-gated Anthem Country Club. For buyers who want Anthem's parks, trails, schools, and community amenities combined with elevated views and a premium feel, Anthem Hills delivers an excellent combination of lifestyle, location, and relative value.

highlights.body: Elevated hillside positioning provides sweeping panoramas of the Las Vegas Valley, the Strip skyline, and surrounding mountain ranges. Views that lower-elevation Anthem homes cannot match.

highlights.body: Southern hillside section of Anthem with larger lots, upgraded construction, and a premium feel. A step above Anthem Center without the guard-gated pricing.

highlights.body: Full access to all Anthem parks, trails, recreation facilities, and community programming. The benefits of a premier master plan at an accessible price point.

highlights.body: Zoned for well-rated Henderson CCSD schools with access to top-rated private and charter options. One of the strongest school zones in the Anthem community.

name: Summerlin North Area
overview: The Summerlin North area is the original heart of Summerlin, encompassing the earliest-developed villages and neighborhoods of the Howard Hughes Corporation's flagship master-planned community. Established beginning in 1990, Summerlin North includes the Arbors, the Crossing, the Willows, and portions of the Trails and Vistas villages, along with standalone communities and commercial centers that form the northern core of the Summerlin lifestyle.

Homes in the Summerlin North area span an exceptionally wide range, from approximately $400,000 for established single-family homes in the earlier villages to over $1.5 million for larger homes in guard-gated enclaves within the Vistas and Trails. This breadth of pricing makes Summerlin North accessible to a wider buyer base than the newer, more premium Summerlin South and Summerlin West sections.

The maturity of Summerlin North is one of its greatest strengths. Established trees, mature landscaping, proven HOA governance, and decades of community character create a settled, comfortable environment. The village centers, community parks, and trail connections have been refined over 30+ years. TPC Las Vegas, a PGA Tour venue, sits within the Summerlin North area, and the Tournament Players Club and related golf amenities add value and prestige to surrounding properties.

For buyers seeking the Summerlin lifestyle at a more accessible price point than the newer western and southern sections, Summerlin North delivers the full Summerlin experience — trails, parks, top schools, community programming, and proximity to Downtown Summerlin — with the added benefit of mature landscaping, established neighborhoods, and proven long-term value. The location also provides the quickest Summerlin Parkway access to the Strip and Downtown Las Vegas.

highlights.title: Original Summerlin Character
highlights.body: The earliest villages of the Summerlin master plan with 30+ years of established community character, mature landscaping, and proven HOA governance.

highlights.title: Wide Price Range
highlights.body: From $400K established homes to $1.5M+ guard-gated estates. The most accessible entry point to the Summerlin lifestyle across the entire master plan.

highlights.title: TPC Las Vegas
highlights.body: Home to TPC Las Vegas, a PGA Tour venue hosting the Shriners Children's Open. The golf course and club add prestige and value to the Summerlin North area.

faqs.q: What is the price range in the Summerlin North area?
faqs.a: Homes in Summerlin North range from approximately $400,000 for established homes in the earlier villages to over $1.5 million for guard-gated properties in the Trails and Vistas.

faqs.q: Is Summerlin North part of the Summerlin master plan?
faqs.a: Yes. Summerlin North is the original section of the Howard Hughes Corporation's Summerlin master plan, encompassing the earliest villages developed beginning in 1990.

faqs.q: What ZIP codes are in Summerlin North?
faqs.a: Summerlin North spans ZIP codes 89134 and 89144 in Las Vegas, Nevada.

faqs.q: What is TPC Las Vegas?
faqs.a: TPC Las Vegas is a PGA Tour venue located in the Summerlin North area. It hosts the annual Shriners Children's Open, a PGA Tour event that brings professional golf to the community.

faqs.q: How does Summerlin North compare to Summerlin South and West?
faqs.a: Summerlin North is the most established section with mature landscaping and lower entry prices ($400K+). Summerlin South features luxury enclaves like The Ridges. Summerlin West is the newest with active new construction. All share Summerlin's amenities.

faqs.q: What schools serve Summerlin North?
faqs.a: Summerlin North is served by top-rated CCSD schools including Sig Rogich Middle School (10/10) and Palo Verde High School (8/10). The Meadows School (A+) and Bishop Gorman (A+) are premier private options.

faqs.q: What are HOA fees in Summerlin North?
faqs.a: HOA fees range from $75 to $350 per month depending on the specific village and neighborhood. The Summerlin master association fee is included, with sub-association fees varying by community.

faqs.q: Is Downtown Summerlin near Summerlin North?
faqs.a: Yes. Downtown Summerlin is approximately 5 minutes from most Summerlin North neighborhoods, offering 125+ shops, restaurants, City National Arena, and the Las Vegas Ballpark.

name: Green Valley Plaza Area
overview: The Green Valley Plaza area encompasses the residential neighborhoods surrounding the Green Valley Plaza shopping center and the broader Green Valley commercial corridor in Henderson. This established area sits at the nexus of Green Valley's retail, dining, and service infrastructure, offering an exceptionally convenient lifestyle for residents who value walkable access to daily needs.

Homes in the Green Valley Plaza area range from approximately $350,000 for condominiums and townhomes to $600,000 for larger single-family homes. The housing stock primarily dates from the late 1980s through the early 2000s, reflecting the original Green Valley master plan's development phases. Floor plans range from 1,200-square-foot attached products to 2,800-square-foot single-family homes, with desert landscaping, mature trees, and established community character throughout.

The area's defining feature is its proximity to Green Valley's commercial core. The Green Valley Plaza, Green Valley Town Center, and surrounding retail strips provide grocery, dining, medical, banking, and professional services within walking distance or a very short drive. Henderson's Recreation Center, Henderson Libraries' Green Valley branch, and multiple neighborhood parks add community amenities that residents value.

For buyers seeking an established Henderson home near shopping and services at a moderate price point, the Green Valley Plaza area provides outstanding convenience. The original Green Valley master plan set high standards for community design, and the neighborhoods surrounding the plaza area reflect those standards with quality construction, well-maintained common areas, and a mature community feel. The area is also well-positioned for commuters with quick access to I-215 and the broader Henderson transportation network.

highlights.title: Ultimate Convenience
highlights.body: Surrounded by Green Valley's commercial core with walkable shopping, dining, medical, and services. Daily errands within minutes or walking distance.

highlights.title: Original Green Valley Character
highlights.body: Part of Henderson's original master-planned community with 30+ years of established community identity, mature landscaping, and proven HOA governance.

highlights.title: Diverse Housing Options
highlights.body: Condos, townhomes, and single-family homes across a wide price range. Something for first-time buyers, families, and downsizers.

highlights.title: Henderson Address
highlights.body: All the benefits of a Henderson address — top city services, low crime rates, excellent parks, and strong property value support.

highlights.title: Commuter Access
highlights.body: Quick access to I-215 for efficient commutes to the Strip, airport, and Summerlin. Central Henderson positioning minimizes drive times.

highlights.title: Established Value
highlights.body: One of Henderson's most proven residential areas with consistent appreciation through market cycles. The Green Valley brand supports long-term property values.
